events.name: The Tui Express
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
The Tui Express
Sunday 3 May 2026
Our Tui Express this May runs the week before Mother’s Day, making it the perfect opportunity to treat Mum to a special day out a little early.
Book here: https://fareharbor.com/embeds/book/mainlinesteamheritagetrust/items/?flow=1555439&full-items=yes&back=https://www.mainlinesteam.co.nz/
Join us as we travel by train to Mangatainoka and the famous Tūī Brewery, enjoying the scenery along the Kapiti Coast and through the Manawatū countryside.
Travel in comfort aboard our premium train, and soak up the atmosphere of classic rail travel.
When we arrive at Mangatainoka, a short bus transfer (or an easy 10-minute walk) will take you to the Tūī Brewery, where you can enjoy a gourmet BBQ lunch in the historic Flagon Room, including a complimentary drink. Each guest also receives a Mangatainoka Passport with vouchers to redeem at the brewery.
Station stops include
Plimmerton
Paraparaumu
Ōtaki
Levin
Palmerston North
It’s a great way to celebrate Mum with a relaxing day out and a unique rail experience.
Seats are limited
Mainline Steam Heritage Trust

events.id: Community Information Day - Upgrading the Cook Strait cable-376 Makara Rd, Makara, Wellington 6972, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Community Information Day - Upgrading the Cook Strait cable
events.venue: 376 Makara Rd, Makara, Wellington 6972, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Transpower is hosting community information days in Makara and Picton about the work we’re doing in the coming years to upgrade Aotearoa’s electricity link between the North and South Islands. This session will be held in Makara.
New Zealand’s High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Link has been in service since 1965. It helps keep our electricity system reliable and stable and allows Kiwis to access the lowest-cost electricity across both islands. It’s a critical piece of our national grid. The current HVDC cables were installed in 1991 and have an expected 40-year lifespan, so they need to be replaced by the early 2030s.
As well as replacing the three existing cables and adding a fourth cable, Transpower’s planned renewal programme includes building new cable termination stations at Oteranga Bay and Ōraumoa/Fighting Bay. This work is expected to start in the summer of 2027/2028.
Construction is still some time away, but we want to share our plans with communities near our sites and hear from them about how we can best understand and manage possible impacts.
Come along to Makara Community Hall on Sunday May 3, 10am-2pm.
Grab a coffee and cookie and meet some of the Transpower team
Learn more about the thinking and planning behind the HVDC Link renewal work
Quiz us on HVDC history (our specialist topic!)
Learn how we will update you on our plans
Bring your questions, feedback and any concerns
We’d love to hear from as many local voices as we can. Please share widely with whānau, friends and local organisations.
events.description:
Transpower is hosting community information days in Makara and Picton about the work we’re doing in the coming years to upgrade Aotearoa’s electricity link between the North and South Islands.
New Zealand’s High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Link has been in service since 1965.
It’s a critical piece of our national grid.
This work is expected to start in the summer of 2027/2028.
Construction is still some time away, but we want to share our plans with communities near our sites and hear from them about how we can best understand and manage possible impacts.

events.id: Ulcerate-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Ulcerate
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Via Debemur Morti:

“After triumphantly casting off the shackles of claustrophobic dissonance on 2020's lauded ‘Stare Into Death and Be Still’, New Zealand unorthodox Death Metal legends ULCERATE up the ante even further with mind-bending 7th album ‘Cutting the Throat of God’.
An acknowledgement that the band's most powerful and affecting material leans to the melodic side, this refined and hook-laden new record is a self-sufficient universe unto itself, a conflagration of inventively visceral Death/Black Metal where dizzying experimentation meets head-nodding abandon - obscenely twisted and addictive, heart-stopping in its depth of feeling.”

To complete the touring cycle for ‘Cutting the Throat of God’, the band will undertake seven Australasian headlining dates in 2026. Special guests for the New Zealand leg are the utterly deadly Vicissitude.

events.id: Money Moves Keynote for Women-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Money Moves Keynote for Women
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Hosted by Wāhine Wanawana - Ara Ki Angitu Charitable Trust & R&P Insurance
Koha only event - please add your koha amount in the space when you are purchasing your ticket.
This keynote event has been built to inspire Wāhine on their financial journey, to build confidence and community in a room full of other Women. Get the gals together and join us for an epic day. Check out R&P Insurance Instagram or website to see the highlights of last year's events in Christchurch.
Wāhine Wanawana - Ara Ki Angitu is a charitable trust set up by R&P Insurance to invest in our community through financial education and connection.
Our Speakers:
Brooke Roberts - Co founder of Sharesies
Sarah Brown - I am not your rehab
Tegan Yorwarth - Mai FM
Te Kahukura Boynton - Māori Millionaire
Courtney Manu - Content creator @themanuss
Rochelle and Philly - R&P Insurance
Our MC - Janelle Riki from Riki Consultancy
Please arrive 10.45am for a strict 11am start. Please use the main entrance to Te Papa and we will direct you to Rangimarie Rooms 1&2 for the event.
You will have time to mingle with each other and our speakers and light refreshments will be provided. We love children but this event is 16+ only and each attendee needs to be registered.
This event is the inspiration, following this we will announce the "practical" side - our financial foundations workshops also to be held in person in Pōneke.

Lucky us, lucky you - No Mames Tacos are taking over the kitchen at GP Leeds on Sunday 3rd May.
No Mames Tacos are so exclusive, they’ve only ever popped-up thrice in Wellington. Even so, they've made a name for themselves as being one of the best street food offerings in town. So, now’s your chance to discover why (if you haven’t already).
For this pop-up at GP Leeds, No Mames are bringing seafood three ways - fresh fish and prawns in a taco trio that would make old mate Poseidon proud.
Sunday 3rd May
From 12pm until sold out
$45 for three tacos + a drink (alcoholic + non-alcoholic options)
No alternate dietary options
Bookings are now open if you want to lock down your tacos - otherwise, just swing by on the day and try your luck.

events.id: Choro Ao Vento-62 Friend St, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Choro Ao Vento
events.venue: 62 Friend St, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for an afternoon of Brazilian choro - a
joyful and expressive music style that began in Rio de Janeiro.
Performed by a small ensemble featuring instruments like guitar,
flute, cavaquinho, clarinet and pandeiro, choro ranges from lively,
fast-paced tunes to slow, lyrical melodies. We rehearse together every
fortnight and regularly collaborate with other musicians at a monthly
roda. This concert is a rare chance to hear us in a dedicated
performance setting, and we’re excited to share it with you.
events.description:
Join us for an afternoon of Brazilian choro - a joyful and expressive music style that began in Rio de Janeiro.
Performed by a small ensemble featuring instruments like guitar, flute, cavaquinho, clarinet and pandeiro, choro ranges from lively, fast-paced tunes to slow, lyrical melodies.
We rehearse together every fortnight and regularly collaborate with other musicians at a monthly roda.
This concert is a rare chance to hear us in a dedicated performance setting, and we’re excited to share it with you.

events.id: Dusty Sunday Social Club-64 Kingsford Smith St, Rongotai, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Dusty Sunday Social Club
events.venue: 64 Kingsford Smith St, Rongotai, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Dusty Sunday Social Club is your new favourite way to close out the weekend - a soothingly sonic safe space for those spirited souls who've pushed the boat out slightly further than expected, only to find there's still some gas left in the tank.
Designed to be the flip side of our Cuba St Tavern series, this is the perfect opportunity to make more memories with the new friends you forgot about making memories with on Friday's dance floor.
No big statements or lofty concepts - barely an advertised line up. Just a finely tuned sound system and a handful of likeminded selectors, embarking on a musical journey that will push their tastes to the outer limits, as they thread the needle between genres, eras, styles and sounds.
We'll be kicking things off from Sunday 3rd May, with a line up of Tempo Schmempo's finest to be revealed on the day.
Free entry.

events.id: He Kaupapa Waka exhibition closing + kōrero-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: He Kaupapa Waka exhibition closing + kōrero
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the story of waka in Aotearoa through powerful photography, carving, and sound.
Before it closes this week, drop in and meet exhibition curator Te Rawhitiroa Bosch and waka experts as they share the stories about the exhibition. This is an incredible chance to learn from the waka community and be surrounded by knowledge carried through generations.
Stay for the karakia whakakapi (closing blessing) – a special kaupapa that is open to the public.
Schedule:
2–3pm: Artist kōrero with Te Rawhitiroa Bosch
3–4pm: Taumata Kōrero: Past, Present, and Future of Waka with Joe Conrad, Stan Conrad, and Robert Gabel, Te Rawhitiroa, Billy Harrison, and Haley Hakaraia. Facilitated by Tamahou Temara.
5–6pm: Karakia Whakamoe
More information and kōrero livestream can be found here: https://www.tepapa.govt.nz/.../he-kaupapa-waka-closing...

events.id: “All seems so lovely because all is home”: The Archaeology of Home-25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: “All seems so lovely because all is home”: The Archaeology of Home
events.venue: 25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
“All seems so lovely because all is home” wrote Katherine Mansfield to Ida Baker, April 1918.
“Home” is both a physical place, and a concept of safety, comfort, and belonging.
In her work as an archaeologist Mary O'Keeffe has explored the idea of “home”: what places and areas in New Zealand have different people called home over the centuries of occupation, and how and why has this changed.
In this illustrated talk, Mary will show the stories and small details revealed by archaeology that shape our ideas of what “home” is.
Part of New Zealand Archaeology Week 2026 and in conjunction with our current exhibition, 'Uncovering Katherine Mansfield's House'. This event is free but, as a charity, donations are always appreciated.
Registration is required due to limited space in the house. Please note that this event will take place upstairs, see the Accessibility page of our website for more information.
You can also attend this event online via Zoom.
Image: Fragments found during archaeological work at Katherine Mansfield House & Garden, reconstructed into a cup. Photo by Kevin Jones.
events.description:
“All seems so lovely because all is home” wrote Katherine Mansfield to Ida Baker, April 1918.
In her work as an archaeologist Mary O'Keeffe has explored the idea of “home”: what places and areas in New Zealand have different people called home over the centuries of occupation, and how and why has this changed.
In this illustrated talk, Mary will show the stories and small details revealed by archaeology that shape our ideas of what “home” is.
This event is free but, as a charity, donations are always appreciated.
Registration is required due to limited space in the house.

events.long_description:
Have you always wanted to unlock the mysteries of reading Tarot?
Are you an experienced reader looking to build on your skills?
Then join me on Sunday 3rd of May as we explore the wonderful world of Tarot. This class will cover all you need to know to understand and begin reading for yourself with CONFIDENCE. Learn about the cards and their meanings, how each card relates to different areas of your life and how to do some simple spreads.
Each guest will receive a detailed workbook of my personal notes and explanations of each card along with a variety of spreads you can try at home. This class is very informative, interactive and loads of fun so bring a friend and enjoy a wonderful afternoon with likeminded souls
When: Sunday 3rd May
Time: 3pm - 6pm
Where: Raumati - address given at time of booking
Cost: $35p/p
To book contact Jody on 02902546443
LIMITED PLACES
Only 10 seats available for this event.

* Please bring along atarot deck, pen and paper for taking notes. If you don't have a deck, I highly recommend purchasing the Rider Waite deck as its imagery is easy to understand

events.id: The Last Stop Before Winter Americana Jam-5 Todman St, Brooklyn, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: The Last Stop Before Winter Americana Jam
events.venue: 5 Todman St, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Hey there all you fine friends, it's that time again! This Sunday we will once again having a gather at the Salty Pidgin in Brooklyn, Wellington, form a circle and share a few songs together. Now I'm not sure it is quite official just yet, but when you see the tops of the Tararuas covered in snow in the distance, you know it's definitely not summer anymore, and even if it's not really winter just yet, it most definitely will be any second now. So, let's try and stave off the cold together and have a jam!
Come along, bring an instrument, bring a new song or dust off an old song that you enjoy, or even just come along to have a listen or a sing and let's set the woods on fire!

events.id: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)-57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)
events.venue: 57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
No pressure, no plan—just new friends

Whether you’re visiting Wellington for the first time, recently relocated, or simply love connecting with curious people, this weekly meetup is the perfect way to share stories, make friends, and go explore, as a group, the magic that Wellington has to offer

What to expect?
• Casual & hostless: no formal host, no schedule: just show up, be yourself, meet fellow solo travellers & expats and decide as a group where you feel like going
• Central meeting point: it is surrounded by cultural landmarks, cafes, and restaurants, making it a convenient and safe starting point.
• Open to everyone: whether you are a local, an expat or just passing through for a few days, you are welcome to join. (English is the primary language, but feel free to switch of course)

How to talk with strangers?
(and create meaningful connections)
To help you get started, I curated a little game of ridiculously good questions you can ask to break the ice, shift the conversation from dull to intriguing and fun, and eventually create meaningful connection quickly. I hope this helps!
www.pierrebonvalot.com/good-questions

The only things you need to do are…
• Leave a message in the comment section to let the others know that you will actually be there (I don’t want you to end up alone there

• If you can’t make it anymore, no problem! But please update your RSVP to let the others know
• Please be gentle and patient with each other. We all want to make good friends here.

Why join?
• Meet open-minded travellers and locals who are keen to connect, learn and have fun together.
• Experience Wellington... as a group.
• Be part of a global community that celebrates solo travelling and meaningful connections between foreign and locals.

How does it works?
1. Arrive at the meeting spot at the scheduled time.
2. Look for other joiners—friendly faces and curious explorers.
3. Introduce yourself, connect with others, and decide as a group where to go.

When & where?
• Day: every Sunday
• Time: 4:00 PM (meetup sometimes displays a different time zone: but it’s always 4pm local time)
• Location: in front of the Albatross Fountain
Hostless: I am setting up the event for you all to meet, but I won't be there myself this time

Hi my name is Pierre
I created this Meetup group because I want to see more human connection happen in the real world. I feel many of us want this, but don't exactly know how to go about it. I hope this helps a little bit.
I also work as a coach: I help people who feel stuck in their life to reconnect with themselves, create real change and ultimately live more fulfilling lives.

www.pierrebonvalot.com/good-questions The only things you need to do are… • Leave a message in the comment section to let the others know that you will actually be there (I don’t want you to end up alone there • If you can’t make it anymore, no problem!

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.name: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026
events.long_description:
What’s tough, fast, and physical? Wellington’s netball team, Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse. After reaching the semi-finals last year, the Pulse are hungry to dominate the ANZ Premiership this year. Their campaign kicks off in April and continues through to June. Five of the ten games are played on home soil, either at the TSB Arena on Wellington’s waterfront, Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua or Fly Palmy Arena in Palmerston North. 
Netball is known for its speed, agility, deft footwork, and pinpoint feeds. As a live spectator sport, it always delivers. This year, Khiarna Williams, Ameliaranne Ekenasio and Ine-Marí Venter provide the fireworks at the shooting end of the court. Head court-side to cheer them on and feel the energy pulse.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.venue: 15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our Out in the Pool sessions are regular monthly sessions for all queer, transgender, gender diverse and non-binary folks
We invite our rainbow communities and allies to dive in and swim in a safe and welcoming environment at Keith Spry Pool. These sessions are held on the first Sunday of every month and run 5pm-7pm, starting slightly later in February with our first event on Sunday 8 February 2026.
Out in the Pool evenings at Keith Spry have:
Gender neutral changing rooms and bathrooms, as well as our Men's and Women's changing facilities.
Recreation lanes with inflatables, as well as the Dive board open (as staffing allows).
Lane space will be available to swim some lengths or aqua jog, and the teaching pool will be open as staffing allows.
Spa and Sauna available
Music, swimming and good vibes only.
There is no additional cost for these sessions, simply turn up and pay our regular pool entry fees. There is no need to register, just come on down!

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.
The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing
this album live is part of that.’
Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.
‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’

The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu.
The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’ Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps.
In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.

events.long_description:
Directed by Edward D. Wood Jr.
Starring Bela Lugosi, Criswell ., Tor Johnson, Vampira .
Residents of California's San Fernando Valley are under attack by flying saucers from outer space. The aliens, led by Eros (Dudley Manlove) and his assistant, Tanna (Joanna Lee), intend to conquer the planet by resurrecting corpses in a Hollywood cemetery.
The living dead -- a cape-wearing ghoul (Bela Lugosi), a vampire (Vampira) and a slow-footed cop (Tor Johnson) who was killed for his nosiness -- stalk curious humans who wander into the cemetery looking for evidence of the UFOs.

events.id: Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga-123 Pirie St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga
events.venue: 123 Pirie St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Looking to build healthy soils, grow healthy kai and take care of the wellbeing of your whānau, whenua and future generations?
Learn to apply hua parakore approaches in your own māra with our very special Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga in May.
The two-day wānanga is led by hua parakore food grower Dr Jessica Hutchings and will take place in Te Whanganui-a-Tara.
Join Jessica to learn how to:
- Reconnect and re-member soil as an ancestor - as Hineahuone.
- Explore how hua parakore supports soil health beyond productivity by focusing on balance, vitality and intergenerational care.
- Learn the six key hua parakore principles and how these inform nurturing soils without synthetic inputs.
- Understand how to observe environmental signs and work in alignment with natural and seasonal cycles.
- Reflect on your own relationships with soil and place.
- Understand how hua parakore can be applied within marae, community and whenua-based contexts to support seed, soil and food sovereignty.
For just $40, you’ll be able to join Jessica for two days, as well as enjoy kai and a resource kete.
When: Saturday 2 May 10-4pm and Sunday 3 May 10-3pm.
Where: Inner Most Gardens, Mt Victoria, Te Whanganui-a-Tara.
Spaces are limited to just 35 participants.

events.venue: Otaki
events.long_description:
Have you been told you have prediabetes?
Are you wondering what you can do to help manage it? If so, the MHT Diabetes Trust can help.
In this FREE 2 hour course you will learn:
- What is prediabetes
- How you can manage it with food, activity and lifestyle choices
- How you can reduce your risk of developing type 2 diabetes.
Please register by visiting our website www.diabetestrust.org.nz and select BOOK A COURSE, then select the Pre-Diabetes label and click on the course you would like to attend. Complete your booking by selecting BOOK NOW. It is important to register so that we know how many participants there are so that we can provide adequate resources and catering.
We look forward to seeing you there!

events.id: Baxter Dury tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Baxter Dury tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Following the release of his latest album, UK alternative artist Baxter Dury is bringing his Allbarone Tour 2026 to Australia and New Zealand. With genre-blurring music that blends post-punk, indietronica and art-rock, his sound leans on sleek synths and a cool, understated aesthetic rooted in a distinctly British sensibility. ‘Allbarone’ can be described as a nine-track tour-de-force, stripping everything away and building Baxter’s most melodically direct, futuristic collection yet.
The Australian leg of the tour will kick off at Melbourne’s Forum on April 28, before heading to Roundhouse in Sydney on April 29, and concluding at The Tivoli in Brisbane on May 1. Dury will then head to New Zealand for performances at The Hollywood Avondale in Auckland on May 3 and Wellington’s Meow Nui on May 4.

events.id: Mapping Wellington: "Mr Ward and the Other Chaps" | Archaeology Week 2026-Antrim House, 63 Boulcott St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Mapping Wellington: "Mr Ward and the Other Chaps" | Archaeology Week 2026
events.venue: Antrim House, 63 Boulcott St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Historian Elizabeth Cox will talk about the wonderful plan of Wellington created by surveyor Thomas Ward in the late 19th century.
“Ward documented hotels, theatres, oyster saloons, brothels, shops, stables, Parliament, prisons, the ‘lunatic asylum’, the hospital, and remnants of Māori kāinga.”
Archaeologist Mary O’Keeffe will also talk about other 19th century survey plans and how they can add context for archaeologists.
LOCATION: Antrim House (63 Boulcott Street, Wellington 6140)
DATE: Monday, 4 May
TIME: 5.30pm
COST: Free event (book via link)

events.venue: 7 Leeds St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Monday 4th May marks the launch of NZSL Week 2026, and this year we’re celebrating 20 years of New Zealand Sign Language’s recognition as an official language in Aotearoa.
Join the teams from Deaf Aotearoa and Garage Project at GP Leeds St as we officially kick off the week. GP has brewed a beer especially - come down to drink it, raise a few toasts and generally have a good time in the name of sign.

We can’t wait to see you there!
From 7pm
Monday 4th May
GP Leeds St
NZSL Week with GP

events.id: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)
English in the Community courses help you to learn more English, get to know your community and meet new people. The groups aim to help you use what you already know, learn more from your tutor and the other students and feel more at home in your local community. 
To enrol in these courses, please contact the CEC office on 04 385 8919 or cec@whs.school.nz.
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Feel more confident speaking English in everyday situations 
• Be able to understand more English 
• Have learned new words and basic grammar
• Know more about living in New Zealand and your local community
The courses are free for NZ residents and citizens. The course fee for non-residents is $155.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
This is an introduction to the wonderful, organic material that is clay. You'll receive a comprehensive overview to working with clay which will cover all steps from clay preparation and re-cycling through to glazing the finished work. You'll learn to throw basic forms with group demonstration and one-to-one instruction.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
•  Wedge and re-cycle clay 
•  Demonstrate the basics of working on a pottery wheel 
•  Centre clay on the wheel 
•  Open a centred piece of clay on wheel 
•  Pull clay up to form basic beaker/cylinder shape 
•  Demonstrate the correct use of prepared glazes 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

For those who can understand and use simple English in everyday situations but need to build confidence, vocabulary and grammar.

• Feel more confident speaking English in everyday situations
• Be able to write more correct sentences
• Be able to understand more English, especially “New Zealand English”

events.id: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
Learn how to prepare and alter clay:  pinch, coil, slab, extruder and roller use.  Understand shrinkage and clay care, joining techniques, drape and press moulding, mark making, slip making and application - as well as tetrahedron, cone and cylinder production
The techniques learnt during the making will provide an excellent springboard to creating a wide array of ceramic objects in the future.
The emphasis will be on learning techniques and honing those skills and by the end of the 8 sessions you will have completed three works which will be fired and glazed.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link.

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
A follow-on course for those who have some experience of throwing on the wheel and would like some guidance to take their skills to the next stage.
You’ll learn how to “turn” your pots with more confidence and be able to make a variety of small to medium-sized projects. Basic glazing of your pots will also be covered.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Turn a pot
• Make a lidded container
• Make a rounded vase
• Form a reasonable sized bowl
• Do basic glazing
All materials included in course fee. Wear old clothes and footwear suitable for a workshop environment and bring an old towel.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.

events.id: Identity group show-148 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Identity group show
events.venue: 148 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We would like to warmly invite you to our "Identity group show" opening night is May 5th, Tuesday at 5:30 pm. Nibbles and drinks provided.
The artists have worked in a variety of different mediums, collage, clay, paint and drawing media to explore, express what identity means for them. Come and celebrate with the artists

Identity is the way we perceive and express ourselves. Factors such as race, ethnicity, or gender-often play a role in defining our identity. However, many aspects of our identities change throughout our lives. Our experiences can alter how we see ourselves or are perceived by others.
Conversely, our identities also influence the decisions we make: Individuals choose their friends, adopt certain fashions, and align themselves with political beliefs based on their identities. Many artists use their work top express, explore, and question ideas about identity.

This is stunning to watch and even more fun to dance! It is based on the original Lambada dance but adapted to more modern music with a slow-quick-quick beat. It can be danced to a wide variety of music including R&B, Rock and Pop.
In this block of 5 classes you will discover the fundamental techniques, movements and sequences of Brazilian Zouk to get you up and running on the dance floor.
This includes:
Week 1: Passo basico + elastico push
Week 2: Lateral
Week 3: Elástico upright (lead walking around)
Week 4: Abertura e giro
Week 5: Combination
They are designed so you can start this block as a complete newbie, or you can continue on as an existing student from the last block.
There are 4 blocks of classes (A, B, C, D) in the complete Fundamentals course. Once these are done, you can progress to the Improvers classes.
Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week term cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students.
- Ad hoc cost is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25
Details: Your name and ZoukFunC

events.id: Introduction to Nichiren Buddhism: 5-week course-287 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Introduction to Nichiren Buddhism: 5-week course
events.venue: 287 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hi!

Join us for a free 5-week introductory course where you can learn about the teachings of Nichiren Buddhism and the practice of chanting Nam-myoho-renge-kyo
Tuesdays 7–8pm from 5 May to 2 June
You’re welcome to join all 5 sessions or just pop in for 1–2!
Everyone is welcome to join!
At this introductory course you’ll have the opportunity to participate in group discussions, hear people share their experiences of this Buddhist practice, and connect with like-minded individuals seeking happiness.
Topics include

5 May – Meaning of Nam-myoho-renge-kyo
12 May – 10 Worlds & Absolute Happiness
19 May – The Gohonzon
26 May – Changing Karma & Oneness of self and the environment
2 June – Inner transformation & striving for the happiness of self and others, and for world peace
Don’t miss this opportunity to explore the teachings of Nichiren Buddhism and take a step toward a more fulfilling life
We look forward to seeing you there!
Feel free to message us with any questions
About Us:
The Soka Gakkai International (SGI) is a community-based Buddhist organisation promoting peace, culture, and education, centred on respect for the dignity of life. With members in 192 countries and territories
SGI members strive to bring out their potential while contributing as empowered global citizens to their communities
Whether you’re new to Buddhism or have some experience, our SGI community offers a warm, supportive space to explore this philosophy and discover a path to inner transformation.
Nam-myoho-renge-kyo!

events.id: All about our ancient bats--41.267300, 174.759000-Facebook
events.name: All about our ancient bats
events.long_description:
A creature of the forest floor and the night sky, the New Zealand short-tailed bat is found nowhere else on Earth and is clinging on to survival in a handful of scattered sites.
Join Dave Hunt as he explores the current conservation status of these extraordinary ancient bats, and the work underway to protect and manage their fragile populations.
Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. Gold coin donation. No need to book, just turn up!
This seminar is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual seminar series. This year, they are on the first Tuesday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.
Image credit: New Zealand lesser short-tailed Bat, Mystacina tuberculata tuberculata, collected 20 June 1998, The Valley, Codfish Island, New Zealand. Gift of Department of Conservation, 1998. CC BY 4.0. Te Papa (LM001330)

Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre.
Gold coin donation.
This year, they are on the first Tuesday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community.

events.id: Auditions - Accidental Death of an Anarchist-110 Cashmere Ave, Khandallah, Wellington 6035, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Auditions - Accidental Death of an Anarchist
events.venue: 110 Cashmere Ave, Khandallah, Wellington 6035, New Zealand
events.long_description:
This is a recent translation and adaptation of the famous farce written in 1970 by Italian writers Dario Fo (Nobel Prize in Literature 1997) and Franca Rame. Accidental Death of an Anarchist is based on a true story – the death of Guiseppe Pinelli, an Italian anarchist, during a police investigation in 1969. This adaptation updates the story to 2020s London.
There are seven roles, with three for men, two for women and three for any gender, including the lead role of The Maniac.
Find out more and register for an audition at https://kat-theatre.org.nz/auditions

events.id: Zach & Lesa Bought a Gun || NZ Intl Comedy Festival-30 Allen St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Zach & Lesa Bought a Gun || NZ Intl Comedy Festival
events.venue: 30 Allen St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A classic double-header performed by two of the most [figure out technically accurate adjective] comedians in NZ, Zach Mandeville and Lesa MacLeod-Whiting.
From motherhood to the CIA’s involvement in so-called “UFO encounters”, they’ll discuss it all! They will not discuss the gun.
They will, however, delight the audience in a metaphorical 9-month all-expenses-paid Alaskan cruise of laughs. This is a show meant for lovers of standup comedy, who delight in the unexpected and well-crafted.
From the creators of Airhorn!
Winner - Best Innovation - 2025 Wellington Comedy Awards
Winner - Best Regular Show - 2025 Wellington Comedy Awards

events.id: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Elementary (ESOL Level 2)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Elementary (ESOL Level 2)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
English in the Community: Johnsonville - Elementary (ESOL Level 2)
English in the Community courses help you to learn more English, get to know your community and meet new people. The groups aim to help you use what you already know, learn more from your tutor and the other students and feel more at home in your local community. 
To enrol in these courses, please contact the CEC office on 04 385 8919 or cec@whs.school.nz.
By the end of the course, you'll: 
• Be more confident in speaking English in everyday situations
• Be able to understand more English, especially “New Zealand English”
• Have increased your vocabulary
• Be able to talk more about living in New Zealand and your local community
The courses are free for NZ residents and citizens. The course fee for non-residents is $155.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
Come along and learn how to create wonderful Vietnamese dishes bursting with fresh flavours. 
Depending on what's seasonally available, dishes taught may include some of the following: green papaya prawn salad (Nộm tôm đu đủ xanh), lemongrass chicken stir fry with steamed rice (Cơm Gà rang sả) , grilled pork noodle and fried rolls (bún nem thịt nướng) and beef noodle soup (Phở bò). 
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya
Thai cuisine is an exciting explosion of tastes and textures. This highly aromatic food has become popular around the globe. Each week you will be shown different Thai dishes and you will get to make these. This course will provide you with tips, tricks and knowledge and it will be fun, easy and yummy!
Not suitable for vegetarians.  All ingredients included in course fee – please bring to each session your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Make a range of Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients spices and herbs like Thai people do!
Be able to explain some simple Thai eating etiquette
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sewing - All Levels-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sewing - All Levels
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sewing - All Levels
All levels welcome! Beginning sewers will learn basic skills and techniques and those with some experience will improve their skills and learn tips and tricks.
Beginners - If you're a beginner, you'll start by making a lined bag, which teaches all the basic skills involved. After that you'll move on to a project of your choice, in consultation with the tutor.  Please bring: 1 metre of firm cotton fabric for your bag outer and 1 metre of a lighter cotton fabric for the lining. You'll also need to bring scissors, a Bernina bobbin (this can be purchased from the CEC office on the day of the course for $7), pins, thread to match your outer fabric, an 18 cm zip and tape measure to the first session.
 If you're a returning student, or have already mastered the basic skills, you'll work on your own project so please bring along whatever you're currently working on (or something new) and your sewing kit. 
Please note PDF patterns are not suitable for this course unless you are very experienced in using them;  we strongly recommend patterns from the following companies:  Simplicity, Butterick, New Look or Quicksew.
By the end of the course, you'll have been introduced to basic skills in dressmaking, if beginners, or further skills for the more experienced.
You'll be able to:
• Use all sewing machine equipment and demonstrate basic sewing techniques (basic seams and finishes)
• Begin to read a commercial pattern and select appropriate materials for task
• Cut out and construct a garment using techniques learned
• (In addition), use a variety of advanced sewing techniques e.g. rolled hems, various zip insertions (invisible and standard), make buttonholes or line garments. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $110 with ID, all others pay $205).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Have a basic understanding of the Māori spiritual and natural worlds (mana atua, mana whenua), and our important connections to them
• Identify the sounds of Māori letters and pronounce letter combinations using correct intonation and stress patterns
• Confidently greet people, introduce yourself and communicate basic personal information in an informal context

events.id: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings
In this exciting beginners course you'll be guided to make a set of 3 cast rings embedded with precious gemstones!  The course follows similar skills and steps as our Lost Wax Cast Rings class, plus a range of sapphires, rubies & other robust stones to add to your rings, 'cast-in-place'.
This beginner-friendly jewellery class is also fun and engaging for returning students to diversify their skills and set stones in new ways.
All materials are supplied in your booking so you won't end up with surprise costs.
Included in your course:
Use of a range of jewellery wax types to create 2 practice rings then 1  main project ring
5 facet-cut precious gemstones to set across your 3 rings (choose from rubies, spinels and sapphires in a range of colours)
Industry standard casting services that we will send your wax models away to so they can be transformed into brass, bronze and sterling silver rings
2 original practice rings cast in bronze or brass
1 main project ring cast in Sterling Silver
During sessions 1-3 you will be guided to sculpt a range of wax ring models and embed precious gemstones, finishing one ring each session ready for casting. 
Between classes your waxes will be transformed into rings for you. You can choose from bronze or yellow brass for your first two rings. These first 2 rings are a great opportunity to practice techniques and experiment with shapes, textures and designs before making your main project ring which will be cast in sterling silver!
In the second half of this course you will discover techniques to trim casting sprues, file, sand, refine and polish your casts into finished jewellery.
The bold, organic forms and textures of the lost wax processes offer a wide range of creative possibilities beyond the scope of fabrication methods alone (soldering metal sheet and wire). Setting faceted stones in wax offers a simpler and more experimental way of adding stones to jewellery compared to fabrication settings. methods. Casdt jewellery techniques and fabrication jewellery techniques can also be combined to great effect, which makes this course a fantastic option for beginners and experienced makers alike.

All materials are included in the course fee. Please bring pen and paper and your reading glasses (if you use these) and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.

By the end of the course you will be able to:
Evolve your jewellery making abilities with new and exciting klost-wax methods
Design and create original sculptural ring designs
Carve hard jeweller’s wax ring master designs to fit a chosen finger size for casting
Sculpt soft jeweller’s wax master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting
Form soft jeweller’s wax to create unique designs ready for casting
Set faceted gemstones in wax for ‘cast-in-place’ setting methods
File, sand and polish metal casts into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Preschool & Year 1 Info Session & Morning Tea-53 Hobson St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Preschool & Year 1 Info Session & Morning Tea
events.venue: 53 Hobson St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
An Exceptional Beginning Starts at QMC's Preschool
At Queen Margaret Preschool, we believe the early years set the foundation for a lifetime of learning, curiosity, and confidence.
Set in our inner‑city campus with leafy grounds, Queen Margaret Preschool offers a small, friendly setting for girls and boys aged 3 and 4, with close relationships between children, teachers, and whānau at the heart of everything we do.
Our passionate, highly qualified educators create a warm, nurturing environment where every child is known, valued, and supported. We proudly draw on two world‑class learning frameworks, Te Whāriki and the International Baccalaureate Primary Years Programme (PYP) combining play‑based inquiry, teacher‑guided learning, and a holistic focus on each child’s development.
__
Join our next Preschool & Year 1 Info Session & Morning Tea
Wednesday 6 May
REGISTER HERE: https://www.qmc.school.nz/events/year-1-information-session/
__
“Our Preschool is committed to providing the highest quality education as your child prepares for the transition to primary school and they continue on their lifelong learning journey.” - Emily Domican, Head of Preschool
“Queen Margaret Preschool is an amazing start to your child’s journey in education. The staff are so dedicated and consistently go above and beyond, always exceeding our expectations.” - Current Parent.

events.name: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $110 with ID, all others pay $205).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Discuss our responsibilities as a bi-cultural nation under ‘Te Tiriti o Waitangi’ and identify underlying Māori values such as: whanaungatanga, pepehā and tikanga
• Discuss the significance of whakapapa as a fundamental Māori world view and confidently describe your family and relationships through this lens
• Form simple sentences to describe parts of your face and body
• Discuss protocol on the marae
• Recognise the importance and use of karakia and be able to recite one correctly
• Recognise the cultural values inherent within different whakataukī (proverbs) and kīwaha (idioms) and be able to correctly recite one of each

events.id: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
During their recording career, Split Enz released a string of hits across the late 70s and early 80s. Those songs quickly gained notoriety and defined an era for many New Zealanders. Now, for the first time in nearly 20 years, the band has reunited for a New Zealand tour. Featuring original members Tim Finn, Neil Finn, Eddie Rayner and Noel Crombie, the Split Enz — Forever Tour will remind audiences of the band’s huge creative talent. Songs include I Got You, I See Red, Message To My Girl, Six Months In A Leaky Boat, History Never Repeats, One Step Ahead, and plenty more. 
Formed in New Zealand in 1972 before relocating to Australia in 1975, Split Enz is widely regarded as one of the most important antipodean groups of the last five decades. Their number one, 1980 album release, “True Colours”, achieved multiple-platinum status in Aotearoa and was certified five times platinum in Australia, selling over 250,000 copies. In 2005, Split Enz were inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame. Says founding member Tim Finn — “Aotearoa, rugged individual! We’re looking forward to climbing into our new Noel Crombie suits and seeing you all soon.”

events.id: Thundercat tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Thundercat tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Bench Music
Known for his jazz-fusion sensibility and elastic funk, Thundercat is bringing his 2026 tour to Wellington this May, performing live in support of his new album Distracted. See his show at Meow NUI in Wellington on Wed, 6 May 2026.
In an age of endless notifications and fractured attention, Thundercat turns the chaos inward on Distracted, his long-awaited new album and one of his most resonant statements yet. Six years on from It Is What It Is, the record captures the restless aftermath of grief and modern overload, balancing existential weight with Thundercat’s unmistakable wit and warmth. It’s an album that laughs through the noise, folding jazz-fusion virtuosity, elastic funk and late-night introspection into songs that feel like unfiltered inner monologues: tender, funny, and painfully human.
That philosophy carries directly into the live experience. Onstage, Thundercat treats Distracted not as a lecture on modern life, but as a shared release, stretching songs, breaking the fourth wall, leaning into humour and spontaneity as a form of survival.
Thundercat's New Zealand shows promise moments of presence inside the noise: music that drifts, refocuses, and reminds you why joy still matters. It’s playful, loose, deeply human, and a rare chance to experience Distracted and all of Thundercat the way it was meant to be felt, not scrolled past.
Thundercat - 'I Did This To Myself (feat. Lil Yachty)' (Official Audio)
Thundercat - 'I Wish I Didn't Waste Your Time'
Thundercat - 'No Moe Lies (feat. Tame Impala)' (Official Visualizer)

events.long_description:
Deaf Aotearoa and Deaf Studies Research Unit at Victoria University Wellington invite you to join us for a lecture followed by Q&A by World Federation of the Deaf President Dr Joe Murray.
This is an exceptionally rare opportunity to see Dr Murray present in person in Aotearoa New Zealand. His work has shaped education policy across the globe, and this lecture offers our community direct access to expertise and insight that is seldom available on our shores.
The event will have interpreters.

events.id: Wednesday Lost Wax Casting, 6 weeks-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Wednesday Lost Wax Casting, 6 weeks
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A fantastic course that's full of fun and inspiration! It starts on Wed 6 May and runs 6 weeks until Wed 10 June , 5.30pm-8pm.
Create interesting designs in wax then send them off to be professionally cast in metal. Once your pieces are back you will be taught how to cut, file and shape them into wearable pieces.
Level 1 students: Learn to work in both soft and hard jewellers' wax and send your waxes away to be cast. When they return you will learn how to clean them up into finished jewellery and/or incorporate them into jewellery in multiple ways. We start with rings but encourage students to think outside the box!
Level 2 students: Advance your carving skills and learn to set stones in your waxes. This can be done either by creating claws and other settings or trying the cast-in-place technique - an organic, exciting process.
Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.
Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what they want to make. Small castings can cost around $7 - $10 and metals are between $0.35 cents per gram for bronze - $4 per gram for silver. Please see details on our Regular Classes page.
Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, PayPal or Afterpay on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this
Course fee $355, early bird $325 till 12 April.

events.id: New Zealand String Quartet presents: Origins | Wellington-74 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: New Zealand String Quartet presents: Origins | Wellington
events.venue: 74 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The New Zealand String Quartet presents their 2026 Season: Storytellers.
Across a three-part Wellington series, we'll explore three distinct programmes: Origins, Transform and Legend. We’ll boldly explore more compositional voices than ever before, beginning with classical masterworks that resonate through the centuries, through to modern-day Grammy winners and the distinctive voices of Aotearoa. The three string quartets of Benjamin Britten form the heart of our 2026 Season, to mark the 50th anniversary of the composer’s death.
Storytellers showcases a rich cross-section of our society and celebrates the art of storytelling through music. Join us in 2026 as together we explore these musical stories.
Programme for Origins:
Mika Cornelius - Universal Veil
Franz Schubert - String Quartet No. 10, in E flat major (D. 87)
Gareth Farr - Mondo Rondo
Interval
Missy Mazzoli - Death Valley Junction
Benjamin Britten - String Quartet No. 1, in D major (Op. 25)
‘Origins’ opens with a piece composed especially for the NZSQ: Mika Cornelius’s Universal Veil, an ethereal and atmospheric trip through the lifecycle of fungi.
Schubert’s vibrant E flat major quartet shows the teenage composer finding his voice before Gareth Farr brings us home with the infectious personality of Mondo Rondo.
Grammy nominee and ‘Brooklyn’s post-millennial Mozart’ (Time Out New York) Missy Mazzoli reimagines a harsh desert landscape into something wild and buoyant in Death Valley Junction.
Origins concludes with the cornerstone of our 2026 Season, a tribute to Benjamin Britten as we play all three of his string quartets in the 50th year after his death. His first string quartet, written at 27 years old, prompted musicologist Peter Evans to declare him “a master of tonal architecture with scarcely a rival on the English scene.” It spans the intimate, restless and sublime and forms the perfect ending to our meditation on beginnings.
Explore the rest of the Wellington series:
Transform | Wednesday 12 August, 6:30pm | Prefab Hall, Wellington
Legend | Wednesday 28 October, 6:30pm | Prefab Hall, Wellington

Techno Echo is a meet-up for women and gender minorities to learn and share about audio and visual practices and technology. Featuring special micro performances and demonstrations by local artists/musicians followed by time for kōrero and connection.
Our first session for this year is a music gear show and tell: a relaxed hangout for femmes and non-binary musicians curious about electronic music.
In this session, Jess (@dreamchambers), Lora (@junipermay_), Celina (@synthethicchildren), and Rara (@musikhara) will each share a favourite piece of gear and how they use it in their practice. You’re also warmly invited to bring along your own favourite gear to share with the group!
Rāapa (Wednesday) 6 May 2026, 7pm - 9pm�
For more info, email technoecho3000@gmail.com or follow us on Instagram: @_technoecho
Hosted by Jess, Lora, Rara, and Celina as part of Pyramid Club’s programme.
Koha entry.
Thanks to Creative New Zealand and Wellington City Council for supporting Pyramid Club's programme.

events.id: John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
BANISHED MUSIC & STRANGE NEWS PRESENT
JOHN MAUS ~ NEW ZEALAND TOUR 2026
WEDNESDAY 6 MAY - MEOW, WELLINGTON
with CROSSING WATER
THURSDAY 7 MAY - DOUBLE WHAMMY, AUCKLAND
with POWER NAP
Tickets from Banished Music
The musical career of John Maus has been riddled with iconic moments. Now, to celebrate the release of his brand new album Later Than You Think, the synth-tastic American songwriter with the unmistakable baritone is headed to New Zealand for two extraordinary shows next May. Brought to you by Banished Music and Strange News, Mr Maus is set to make good on a long-held promise.
In 2019, after a series of personal tragedies and the stresses of touring his high-intensity show left Maus feeling unable to perform here, he flew home urgently to recover, with shows here cancelled at short notice. Now, with five years of contemplation, retreat and recuperation, and time spent crafting his new record, John Maus is set to make his return.
Throughout his long career, Maus considered himself to have been “constantly fighting the status quo: the Mickey Mouse Club, Coca-Cola, and all the institutions that reduce culture to its lowest common denominator”. Then, in 2020, he was infamously photographed at the January 6 riots, and was himself promptly “cancelled”.
In hindsight, he says, he now knows he should have been a lot clearer about his ethos and political beliefs in the wake of it all. “I thought my legacy would speak for itself,” Maus told Stereogum in a recent interview. Stating he was only at the protest to shoot footage for a new documentary film alongside film-maker Alex Moyer, these days he realises he ought to have stated his position a lot more bluntly. “I should have been clearer that I’m absolutely against Trumpism,” he says. “It wasn’t as forthright a denunciation as it should have been.”
Powered by “genuine emotion and radical sincerity”, the new album includes I Hate Antichrist, a song Maus describes as a sort of sequel to Cop Killer, and features a secular Gregorian chant that embraces French philosopher Alain Badiou’s anti-xenophobic sentiment that “All the people who are here are from here”.
From the death of ego and descent into madness, this is a party we’re all invited to.

events.long_description:
Drop in on your morning ride for coffee, a bite to eat, and a chat with fellow riders. This relaxed commuter stop is a simple way to connect with others travelling through the eastern suburbs.
Whether you’re riding to work, heading to school drop-off, or simply out for a morning spin, swing by Uncle Leo’s.

It’s also a great chance to get involved in Bike Buddies, whether you’re new to riding and keen for a bit of support, or an experienced rider who’d love to help someone else build confidence getting around the city.
Find out more about Bike Buddies Wellington on Facebook or email them at bikebuddieswellington@gmail.com

events.id: Leadlighting/Stained Glass-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Leadlighting/Stained Glass
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Leadlighting/Stained Glass
Bring along your project ideas and learn the techniques of glass cutting and making stained glass panels. Suitable for both beginners and those with previous knowledge.
NOTE: If this is your first Leadlighting course, you MUST attend the first session when key instruction is given. If you do not attend the compulsory first session, you will forfeit your course fees and will not be able to transfer to a future session of this course
(Unsuitable for pregnant people due to presence of lead in some materials)
 Your materials costs are dependent on the size of your project and the coloured glass content (likely minimum cost around $170). Please wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.
By the end of the course, you'll: 
• Have designed and constructed a small rectangle/window, lampshade or light catcher 
• Be able to cut glass and assemble leadlighting panels 
• Be able to solder, seal and finish a leadlighting project 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Chinese Mandarin - Introductory 2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $105 with ID, all others pay $205).
For those who have completed an Introductory 1 course (16 hours) or have equivalent knowledge (e.g. who may have picked up some basic survival language through travel).

events.id: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
We will prepare simple, traditional Japanese home-style meals consisting of rice, soup, and a main dish (Ichijyu-issai). In each class, we'll make a different-flavoured miso soup along with a popular Japanese dish -- for example, teriyaki chicken or tonkatsu (crumbed pork cutlet).
  We'll introduce you to Japanese food that's easy to prepare, tasty, and made with simple fresh ingredients plus we'll show you how easy it is to incorporate tasty and healthy Japanese food into your daily diet.
All ingredients are included in the fee. You just need to bring an apron, a dish cloth, a tea towel, and a container to take your food home in. Don't forget your smartphone if you want to take photos!
By the end of the course, you’ll:
become more familiar with using Japanese ingredients
feel more confident to cook authentic Japanese-style dishes at home 
have the know-how to make delicious, well-balanced, and nourishing meals for the whole family.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus. 
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Creative Writing - Express Yourself-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - Express Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - Express Yourself
Creative writing is a powerful tool for personal growth and is an art form that belongs to all people. Everyone has their own unique writer’s voice, so if you’ve always wanted to explore your creativity through writing, without pressure, join this weekly workshop.
  No previous writing experience needed - express yourself! Bring pen and paper and/or your laptop.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Unlock your hidden creativity
• Explore your writer’s voice
• Experience personal insights
• Develop self-awareness through writing
• Produce text with confidence
• Write on 
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers' work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Spanish - Introductory 2
For those who have done Spanish Introductory 1 (16 hours) or have equivalent knowledge (e.g. who may have picked up some basic survival language through travel).
By the end of the course, you'll:
• Have an improved understanding of, and ability to use more successfully, the material from the previous course 
• Have some improved ability to express yourself in basic Spanish 
• Have some understanding of present tense irregular verbs, and be able to use some of them correctly 
• Understand how comparisons are formed and be able, with some success, to use them correctly 
• Have some understanding of how pronouns work, and be able, on some occasions, to use them correctly 
• Be able to use, successfully at times, the Spanish for "this", "that", "these" and "those"

events.id: Wellington Alumnae Lunch-Shed 5 Queens Wharf, Wellington, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Alumnae Lunch
events.venue: Shed 5 Queens Wharf, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join SMCOGA President Sophie Fitzsimons and Alumnae Manager Amanda Marchant for the 2026 Wellington Alumnae Long Lunch.
2026 Speaker: Marcy Banbury (Class 2003)
Creative Director at New Zealand Rugby.
Marcy will take us behind the scenes of one of New Zealand's most iconic brands, exploring how storytelling and visual identity connect fans to the game and the team. She'll share what it means to be a creative leader at NZ Rugby, how the rise of the Black Ferns has expanded and evolved the way NZR expresses itself creatively, and what she's learned stepping into a senior creative role in the world of sport.
Date: Thursday 7 May
Time: 12:00 pm - 2:00 pm
Location: Private Room, Shed 5, Queens Wharf, Wellington
This luncheon promises a delightful opportunity for our alumnae to reconnect, reminisce about school days, and explore shared opportunities of today.
We hope you are able to join us.
RSVP by 29 April
Cost per person: $70
Ticket price includes: Breads, Main and Dessert with complimentary glass of wine or non alcoholic beverage, Tea and Coffee. Further drinks are available on a cash bar basis.

events.id: Musical Punch Over Lunch Concert Series-Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Musical Punch Over Lunch Concert Series
events.venue: Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Now in its sixth year, Old St Paul’s winter concert series returns, championing the depth of talent right here in Te Whanganui-a-Tara!
Each Thursday, local performers take over this one-of-a-kind venue for a burst of live music to lift your day. Set inside the 160-year-old architectural gem that is Old St Paul’s, this is a peaceful, welcoming place to pause and enjoy something special.
It’s a simple proposition: step out of your routine, hear something new, and spend an hour somewhere that feels a world away from the street, and stressors, of outside.
Bring your lunch, take a pew, and enjoy live music in one of Wellington’s most distinctive, and beautiful, heritage spaces. Performances will be up to 45 minutes in duration.
Koha entry.
Programme
May 7: Petit ChouX, French chanteuse
May 14: The Guiding Tones, NZSM jazz trio
May 21: Harbour Capital Chorus, men’s barbershop
May 28: Special guest performance (to be revealed)
June 4: Scots College, choir & chamber group
June 11: Vox Ethno, European folk band
June 18: Tomos Christie & Amelia Lin, NZSM cello/piano duo
June 25: RNZAF band, woodwind quintet
July 2: Mike Hogan, Christopher Hill & Jake Church, Bach on guitars
July 9: Hihi & The Stitchbirds, folk band
July 16: Eastern Echoes Ensemble, classical Chinese
July 23: Wellington City Chorus, women’s barbershop

events.id: Kyla Cobbler — Gone Rogue-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Kyla Cobbler — Gone Rogue
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Known for raw storytelling, razor-sharp crowd work, and unfiltered honesty, Kyla Cobbler commands the stage with total confidence. The comic from Cork in Ireland brings her show ‘Gone Rogue’ to Wellington. In it, Kyla covers everything from the chaos of Barcelona dating life, navigating a friend crush, and the power plays of a barbecue. 
Kyla is one of the fastest-rising names in comedy. She first gained attention through her viral Instagram reels before stepping on stage for her first open mic in 2021. Five years later, Kyla has built a global following, with close to 700K Instagram followers and 100M+ video views. 
Kyla brings quickfire wit and a knack for spinning real-life chaos into killer stories. Get ready for a performance that is electric, unpredictable, and unforgettable.

events.name: End of Season Wrap Party
events.venue: Level 1, 60 Dixon Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us as we celebrate an incredible season of growing, foraging, cooking and sharing food together.
This end of season dinner is a chance to recognise everyone’s incredible mahi, honour what we’ve created together and celebrate the connections that make it all possible
Throughout the evening, we’ll take time to reflect on the season that’s been, sharing stories, ideas and insights from our collective experience. It’s also an opportunity to look ahead, gathering thoughts and inspiration to help shape and strengthen the season to come.
Now more than ever our local food systems and connections to one another are truly worth celebrating.
Everybody Eats- Ltd 60 Dixon Street
Doors open at 6pm, dinner will be served at 6.30
We’ll share a 3 course meal together with drinks available to purchase on the night.
Book your spot so we know how many to cater for,
See you there
