events.id: The Big Bike Film Night 2026 - Wellington-205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Big Bike Film Night 2026 - Wellington
events.venue: 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Let the good times ROLL! The screening sensation known as The Big Bike Film Night is back in '26 for your bike films fix!!!
Sunday 22nd March 5:00pm SOLD OUT
Wednesday 25th March 6:30pm SOLD OUT
Wednesday 1st April 6:30pm SOLD OUT
Thursday 7th May 6:15pm SOLD OUT
Thursday 7th May 7:00pm
Thursday 14th May 6:30pm
What's it all about??? The Big Bike Film Night is on a mission- bringing the best cycling short films from around the world together for you.
Showcasing 2.5 hours of riveting bike films that have everything a cycle-centric audience could want - action, drama, humour, and plenty of inspiration; the evening is unashamedly and utterly, utterly, utterly designed and devised to delight the two-wheel devotee!
Our Promo Trailer
https://bit.ly/Big_Bike_Film_Night_2026_Promo_Trailer
The 2026 treasure chest of films includes:
• a dazzling mountain bike ride exploring the breathtaking landscapes of Norway, embracing the magic of the midnight sun
• a life-changing experience for two adventurous women as they embark to cycle to all the ski-fields in New Zealand in one gigantic attempt
• an inspiring film that captures the wettest, most miserable riding conditions imaginable
• a film that playfully delves into the world of those taking cycling to new heights - celebrating fun, silliness, and creativity that comes with pushing boundaries on two wheels
• a compelling story about perseverance, showcasing the challenges and triumphs of navigating daily life following a life-altering injury, and how adaptive design has transformed and enriched the journey forward
• a moving movie that follows a father and son’s journey as they take on one of life’s most meaningful milestones- riding a bike together as a family
• a bold and uplifting story of an adventure activist, attempting to break the 24-hour Guinness World Record in downhill mountain biking- all to raise funds for reforesting foothills around Queenstown, New Zealand. Her relentless pursuit not only pushes the limits of endurance but also becomes a powerful symbol of resilience and environmental commitment
• and crowning the night off… we all love an underdog tale. This charming, funny, heartfelt film inspires us to believe that we all have the capacity to be unlikely heroes in our own stories. The audience is transported back to the nostalgic 1990s, to the slopes of the punishing Mont Ventoux, in Southern France, to witness the drama of a battle between two giants
ALL in the mix!
These short films are a great reminder of why we love to ride our bike; with stories that captivate us, stories that make us think, and most importantly of all- inspiring us to get out and ride. All celebrate the fun, adventure, and joy that cycling enables.
The New Zealand
󠁿
2026 National Tour is brought to you by our PRESENTING PARTNERS
CYCLE JOURNEYS, and Destination Queenstown | Lake Wānaka Tourism
NATIONAL COMPETITION*
When you buy a ticket to attend a Big Bike Film Night New Zealand you can enter our National Competition to win some incredible prizes
Thanks to our Presenting Partners, Cycle Journeys, you could WIN a day ride for two on the new Kawatiri Coastal Trail, the prize includes 1 night's accomodation in Westport + breakfast
Website https://cyclejourneys.co.nz/kawatiri/
Thanks to our Presenting Partners, Destination Queenstown and Lake Wānaka Tourism you could WIN a biking holiday for two in Queenstown and Wānaka - New Zealand’s ultimate mountain biking and cycling destination.
Website https://www.queenstownnz.co.nz
Website https://www.wanaka.co.nz
Thanks to our Supporters, Ground Effect Cycle Clothing, you could WIN some awesome CYCLING KIT and 6 x $100 gift vouchers
Website https://www.groundeffect.co.nz
*Terms and Conditions apply

PREVIOUS ATTENDEE’S COMMENTS:-
“One of the most inspiring events of the year"
“Wow! If it comes to your town do NOT miss it. Something for everyone.”
“The Big Bike Film Night is always a wonderful, eclectic, wacky mix of films and I love it!”
“Inspiring | Breathtaking | Heartwarming | Quirky | Bonkers | Funny as F##k!"
HEALTH WARNING: Watching this collection may cause you to want to ride for no less than 1 second and no more than 24 hours per day. Potential side effects could also include buying a bike, bike gear, bike clothing, bike travel books, etc., etc., and longing to get lost with your bicycle. In addition to being entertaining, this event will inspire and lead to activities that improve your physical and mental well-being.
FILM CLASSIFICATION: THE BIG BIKE FILM NIGHT is exempt from classification, but the films are suitable for the entire family, and would obtain PG ratings.
Reserving seats on these nights is always tricky so we cannot guarantee specific spots. If you have a strong preference, TEXT 027 590 2117 to reserve your seats ONLY after you have paid for them.
So...listen to the voice inside you; it’s telling you to go. Grab your mates, grab your tickets, GO ON… grab your bike and come along for the ride!!

events.id: DJ Music Production - An Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: DJ Music Production - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
DJ Music Production - An Introduction
Welcome to the world of Electronic Dance Music (EDM). This introductory course will show you the "ins and outs" of producing your own music. Using Fruity Loops SL as the main production program, you'll learn how to compose, arrange, record, edit, mix and master quality music.
While it's designed to complement the "How to be A DJ" course, you can still enrol if you have no experience. 
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Use the basics of the Fruity Loops SL software
- Navigate audio and midi tracks
- Create drum Lines and sequences
- Play basslines and samples
- Render tracks and save formats e.g. mp3, wav etc
- Use basic mixdown and master of music
The course will be held at DJ Shan’s studio at Shop 21, 73 Cuba Street, Wellington.  Bring headphones, a USB and a laptop, if you have one. Limited to eight participants.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Garden Design: Landscaping-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Garden Design: Landscaping
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Garden Design: Landscaping
In this fun and interactive class you will be inspired to find answers to develop a simple makeover or design a grand new plan for your own outdoor area. Find solutions to problems and with creativity, develop your own design to suit your site and character.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
 
• Anticipate what hardscaping is required and draw up a full plan for a garden / outdoor space.
• Identify and take into account your preferred style, wish list and work to site conditions (including location and aspect of the property.)
• Accurately draw your detailed design on paper and to scale.
• Use your drawing to build the garden yourself or use it to consult a builder/landscaper to do the hardscaping on your behalf.
• Identify what materials will produce the style / preferred end result
• Work to a budget or know what range of budget to anticipate
 
Please note that this course DOES NOT include:
 
• Planting design, although inclusion of trees will be considered in part of this course. Planting design is expected to be available in a later course.
• Building the garden
• Learning skills to build your garden
• Recommendation of contractors and suppliers for hardscaping

Please bring some scrap paper, a notebook, pens, pencils and an eraser.
 
It is highly recommended you take photos of the area. Bring these in to discuss in class as “personal case studies”.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings
The lost wax process offers a wide range of creative outcomes such as 3D forms and textures, which are not achievable with fabrication soldering techniques.
You'll get a hands-on opportunity to develop new techniques creating rings with a sculptural and organic flair.
All materials are supplied and included, so you won't end up with surprise "extra" costs.
Included in your course:
Explore using a range of jewellery wax types to create 2 practice rings and 1 X main project ring
Access to industry-standard casting services where we will send your  wax models to be transformed into brass, bronze and sterling silver rings 
2 original practice rings cast in bronze or yellow brass
1 main project ring cast in sterling silver
In sessions 1, 2 and 3 you will be guided to sculpt a range of wax ring models, finishing one each session so it is ready for casting. Between classes your waxes will be transformed into rings for you. You can choose from bronze or yellow brass for your first two rings. These first 2 rings are a great opportunity to practice techniques and experiment with shapes, textures and designs before making your main project ring which will be cast in sterling silver!
In the second half of this course you will discover techniques to trim casting sprues, file, sand, refine and polish your casts into finished jewellery.
This course offers beginners a fun introduction to jewellery making, and for those with some jewellery experience, it offers a great way to advance and expand your making techniques to new levels!
All materials are included in course fee.  Please bring pen and paper and your reading glasses (if you use these) and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.  
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-          Evolve your jewellery making abilities with new and exciting lost wax methods
-          Develop and create unique sculptural ring designs
-          Carve hard jeweller’s wax ring master designs to fit a chosen finger size for casting
-          Form soft jeweller’s wax master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting
-          Combine hard and soft jeweller’s wax to create unique designs ready for casting
-          File, sand and polish metal casts into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Mobile Tickets are like Print-at-Home tickets but instead of having to print off the tickets yourself, you can just show the barcode on your mobile phone. It is the easiest way to access tickets to your events. For more information visit Ticketmaster.co.nz/mobileticket

Please note: There is no elevator in the Opera House and access to the Dress Circle and Gallery are via stairs. The Gallery may be unsuitable for patrons with accessibility requirements or experience vertigo. For further information, please contact us on 04 916 1205.

Kyla is one of the fastest-rising names in comedy. The Cork-born comic first gained attention through her viral Instagram reels, before stepping on stage for her first open mic in 2021. Just four years later, Kyla has built a global following, with close to 700K Instagram followers and 100M++ video views and has established herself as a must-see performer.

events.id: Improv for the Stage-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Improv for the Stage
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Improv for the Stage
Laugh, play characters, get them into trouble (and out again!) and see how your skills translate into confident performance.
This is the WIT Level 2 course and will take your improv that next step beyond the confidence-building of our Joy of Improv, and on into collaborative spontaneous theatre creation.
Suitable for those who have completed a foundation course either with WITor another improv troupe or have some theatrical experience or training.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Create characters and stories in open scenes
• Play and perform several classic improv games
• Stay positive and enjoy failure
• Use improvisation terminology to discuss story, character, comedy, theatre and think about performance (your own and others)
The course will end with a show for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Photography - Beginners
If you want to get the best results from your camera, you need to learn the fundamental skills and theory of good photography. 
Bring your fully manual-capable (D)SLR or mirrorless camera and owner's manual (if you have one) to the first session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Operate your camera in manual mode with more confidence 
• Use your camera controls such as shutter speed and aperture with confidence 
• Determine a correct exposure 
• Present and discuss images in a group environment 
• Demonstrate technical concepts through their practical application
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
NOTE:  Compact cameras are not suitable for this course. Includes a Saturday field trip at a date and time to be advised - check individual course details.

events.id: The Joy of Improvisation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: The Joy of Improvisation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Joy of Improvisation
Improv is theatre without a script and a fun way to build your skills in many areas, including team work, public speaking, storytelling and on-your-feet creative thinking.
This is the perfect place for absolute beginners to get acquainted with the joy and art of improvisation, inspired by the generous creative philosophy of Keith Johnstone. It's also a great place to get back into improv if you've been away from it for a while.
WIT has been teaching this course in association with the CEC for almost 20 years now. It uses fun games and structured exercises to teach the foundation skills of improv. These are a prerequisite for further courses and membership of WIT (www.wit.org.nz), as well as useful in themselves.
If you have questions about the course, please contact WIT directly on info@wit.org.nz.
By the end of the course, you'll have: 
• Explored basic principles of improvisation
• Enjoyed interacting in a friendly, positive and supportive environment
• Developed more confidence, presentation and communication skills
• Experienced joyful moments of improvisation
The course will end with a directed demonstration of class exercises for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

This is the perfect place for absolute beginners to get acquainted with the joy and art of improvisation, inspired by the generous creative philosophy of Keith Johnstone.
WIT has been teaching this course in association with the CEC for almost 20 years now.
It uses fun games and structured exercises to teach the foundation skills of improv.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.

events.id: The Rions Everywhere Every Single Day Tour | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: The Rions Everywhere Every Single Day Tour | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Australian indie-rock four piece The Rions have announced the Everywhere Every Single Day New Zealand tour in support of their debut album Everything Every Single Day. 

Currently midway through their 19-date Australian run, the band are extending the celebration across the Tasman for three consecutive shows this May. They will perform at San Fran, Wellington on Thursday 7 May, Loons, Christchurch on Friday 8 May, and Double Whammy, Auckland on Saturday 9 May. With momentum at an all-time high, these shows welcome Aotearoa fans into The Rions most exciting chapter of their career to date. Tickets go on sale tomorrow, Wednesday 4 March at midday via moshtix.co.nz 

Made up of high school best mates Noah Blockley, Harley Wilson, Asher McLean and Tom Partington, The Rions have rapidly evolved from local Northern Beaches favourites into one of Australia’s most exciting indie-rock exports. They first began gaining traction in 2020, releasing a series of singles that led to their debut EP Minivan in August 2023, followed by Happiness In A Place It Shouldn’t Be in 2024. These early projects were picked up by Australia’s iconic Triple J, with breakout single ‘Night Light’ winning the station’s prestigious Unearthed High competition in 2021 and establishing the foundation for a national following. Their growth has continued to accelerate, translating into relentless touring cycles that quickly found a place in Aotearoa.

Now, the band are in the heat of their success, after the release of their debut full studio album, Everything Every Single Day, released via Community Music in October 2025. The record captures an evolution of their sound, delivering a pensive, contemplative and emotionally-driven collection of pristine indie-rock. It debuted at #1 on the ARIA Australian Artist Albums Chart and stayed familiar to Triple J, taking lead single ‘Tonight’s Entertainment’ to Like A Version, alongside their signature indie-pop rendition of Paramore’s ‘Ain’t It Fun’. 

The Rions - ‘Tonight’s Entertainment’ (Official Video) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=76D3mDGo-0k



Follow The Rions

Spotify | Instagram | Website | Facebook | YouTube | TikTok | Apple Music

events.id: Featherston Booktown Karukatea Festival--Wellington NZ
events.name: Featherston Booktown Karukatea Festival
events.long_description:
The Aotearoa literary landscape is placed front and centre at Featherston Booktown. The annual book festival is now in its eleventh year and expects to attract 10,000 to the south Wairarapa town from 8 to 10 May.
The lineup of writers, raconteurs, and thinkers includes renowned Australian author Hannah Kent, Aotearoa superstar Catherine Chidgey, and activist Tāme Iti. You’ll also find former Olympian and popular reading influencer Gemma Flynn, and former All Blacks mental skills coach Gilbert Enoka. Director Jordan Hamel says he is thrilled to bring the festival into its second decade. “There’s an exciting programme, with bold conversations, new innovations, and an incredible array of national and international talent.”

events.id: Tom Scott tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Tom Scott tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Off the back of the release of his Aotearoa Music Chart topping album ANITYA, Tom Scott brings his self untitled tour to Wellington to play Meow Nui on Friday, 8 May.
Raw as exposed nerves after a car crash, and as tender as the final kiss goodbye, ANITYA is the first solo album from Tom Scott, one of the most acclaimed New Zealand artists of his generation, hip-hop or otherwise. Recorded with a cast of 30 of the most versatile musicians, beatmakers and vocalists in his contact list and the award-winning studio engineer and producer Ben Lawson, it represents a significant creative breakthrough for one of Avondale’s finest. A tale told in two halves, ANITYA is a collect call from the void, offering redemption as you gaze into the house fire. Rendered through a nocturnal blend of progressive soul, dream-pop, jazzy psychedelic folk and ambient R&B, the album’s ten songs confirm Scott’s status as a preternaturally gifted lyricist with an ear for a unifying hook, revelling in the expansive possibilities of who he is still becoming as a songwriter, producer and melodist.
It’s not all pretty, but it’s high art. After all, much like the truth, art can be very ugly.
Born of the blood, sweat, and tears shed with Homebrew, @Peace, Average Rap Band, and Avantdale Bowling Club, Scott’s craft as a music maker and performer is second to none.

Raw as exposed nerves after a car crash, and as tender as the final kiss goodbye, ANITYA is the first solo album from Tom Scott, one of the most acclaimed New Zealand artists of his generation, hip-hop or otherwise.
Recorded with a cast of 30 of the most versatile musicians, beatmakers and vocalists in his contact list and the award-winning studio engineer and producer Ben Lawson, it represents a significant creative breakthrough for one of Avondale’s finest.
A tale told in two halves, ANITYA is a collect call from the void, offering redemption as you gaze into the house fire.

events.id: Kyra Sims & Ryn Hardiman (US) / March of the Kororā-Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Kyra Sims & Ryn Hardiman (US) / March of the Kororā
events.venue: Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
American French hornist Kyra Sims combines electronically processed field recordings from Antarctica with free improvisation, vocals, and storytelling, in collaboration with A/V designer Ryn Hardiman. They will be supported by local multi-instrumentalist duo March of the Kororā.
__
Kyra Sims is a musician, writer, and theatremaker residing in NYC. She has worked with artists such as Rosalía, Jon Batiste, Earth, Wind & Fire, Carole King, and Lizzo, and performed in the Broadway musical Illinoise. Kyra now works independently as a performance artist, combining French horn with vocals, electronics, and spoken word, blending slice-of-life themes with hopeful Afrofuturism.
In January 2026 she traveled to Antarctica as a Polar STEAM fellow on a research vessel, and the work she created on this voyage, Otto in Antarctica, will be performed here at the Pyramid Club!
-
Ryn Hardiman is a Chicago based experimental A/V designer and performer with over 2 decades of experience working in live theatre and devised performance. As a technical designer, Ryn has collaborated with hundreds of artists and dozens of institutions including The Neo-Futurists, Manual Cinema, Blue Man Group, The Art Institute of Chicago, and The Museum of Contemporary Art Chicago. Their artistic practice explores electronic signal manipulation as a performative medium – utilizing a combination of analog and digital equipment to shape, bend, slice, and distort, audio and video signals to create electronically collaged environments.
-
March of the Kororā are a pair of multi-instrumentalists trying to make sense of ecological collapse, injustice and resistance. Born out of jazz jams, space explorations and rage.
https://marchofthekorora.bandcamp.com/

$20 presales via UTR: https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../Kyra-Sims-and-Ryn...

events.name: Late Night Lit: When We Become Stars
events.venue: Burling Flats, 58 Bell St, Featherston 5710, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Poetry allows us to hold the infinite, if only for a moment. Come and experience that moment with Aotearoa's finest, fiercest poets. Galaxies will quiver, rally cries will echo, night owls will hoot and holler as our old selves evaporate into dusk, making way for the new flesh. Emma Barnes, Mary McCallum, and Robert Sullivan (Ngāpuhi, Kāi Tahu) will be wrangled by MC Jordan Hamel. Settle in with a drink under the cover of darkness as these brilliant bards break the silence of the long night.
Please note that due to unforseen circumstances, Matariki Bennett is no longer able to appear at this event.
Generously sponsored by Peter and Mary Biggs.

events.id: Manawa Ora Workshop - The Foundations of Māori Healing and Bodywork-236 Middleton Rd, Churton Park, Wellington 6037, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Manawa Ora Workshop - The Foundations of Māori Healing and Bodywork
events.venue: 236 Middleton Rd, Churton Park, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Manawa Ora Mirimiri Workshop - The Foundations of Māori Bodywork and Healing.
Now - more than ever - there is a focus on self-care and a revitalisation of our traditional healing practices.
This workshop will be held at The Manawa Ora whare hauora (clinic) in Glenside, Wellington.
Date: Saturday 9-10th May
Please note spaces for this workshop are STRICTLY LIMITED.
This is a 2-day workshop only - not a noho-marae (sleepover).
Hours are 9am-4pm, Saturday and Sunday.
Please note this workshop will NOT qualify you as a practitioner.
You will be introduced to Mirimiri and Romiromi; basic protocols and the philosophies that underpin practice, cultural concepts of health and wellbeing and the importance of selfcare.
If you are travelling from out of town please ensure you have made your own accommodation arrangements. Morning teas and lunch provided
The cost of this workshop is $650. Please note spaces are strictly limited. You will need to pay in full when registering.
TO REGISTER: Please email: Joliedavis@manawaora.org
You will need to include information about yourself, where you are from, where you currently live and work and what you do for a living. Please outline the reasons why you want to attend, and what you hope to get out of attending.
FYI if you work in community, education, healthcare or social services you may be eligible to have your registration fees paid for under professional development or cultural training. Please enquire with your employer.

events.id: One Day Secular Meditation Retreat in Wellington-The Inner Mt Victoria, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: One Day Secular Meditation Retreat in Wellington
events.venue: The Inner Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
We know from ancient wisdom and modern psychology that meditation brings many benefits for our mental and physical wellbeing. It can reduce worry and pain, and improve calmness, focus, sleep, relationships and life satisfaction. The more time we spend meditating, and the more skilled we become, the greater the benefits.
So take a restorative break from your daily life, meet other meditators, and strengthen your practice at this small-group Saturday retreat. Everyone is welcome, whatever their level of meditation experience or preferred style. The retreat is beginner-friendly, and our approach is secular (meaning non-religious) which makes room for diverse beliefs.

The programme
We will begin with introductions, followed by a guided meditation that covers the core skills of both sitting and walking meditation. For the rest of the day, the meditation will be unguided apart from occasional bells to announce breaks, and to remind you to alternate between sitting and walking (if that's what you choose to do).
To help quieten the mind, many participants prefer not to talk again until the discussion circle at the end of the retreat. However some people like to chat with others during the breaks, and there is space allocated for this. The retreat leaders are always available for quiet conversations if you need support with your practice.

The venue
The Innermost Gardens hall is nestled between the central city suburb of Mount Victoria and the green town belt. This provides a peaceful nature-rich setting for the retreat, surrounded by community gardens to enjoy during the breaks or walking meditation.
There will be hot drinks and snacks available throughout the day. For lunch the participants bring a plate of vegetarian food to share. The venue has a full kitchen including cutlery, plates, bowls, fridge, stove, and a microwave.

The leaders
Bryan Royds is an adult educator and registered counsellor with a passion for wellbeing science and leadership development. He has been meditating and teaching meditation skills in groups for many years, including the well-researched methods of Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR).
Sarah Jane Barnett is a writer and creative writing teacher at Massey University. She has been a Buddhist for ten years, practicing mostly Vipassana meditation. She also co-facilitates a meditation group for people who practice Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (an evidence-based modern psychotherapy and wellbeing method).
The registration fee is only $20, fully refundable until one day prior. The workshop is limited to 18 participants so register early to reserve your place.

events.venue: 62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Triplets, amusement parks, and controversial scientists are at the heart of two Aotearoa novels longlisted for the Ockham New Zealand Book Awards. Just one of these overlapping elements would have been an amazing coincidence, but all three is nothing short of astonishing. Mary McCallum talks to Catherine Chidgey and Tracy Farr about The Book of Guilt and Wonderland and attempts to unearth what feeds the fertile imaginations that dream up such wonders.
Generously sponsored by Sir Chris and Lady Kathy Parkin.
Please note this event will now take place in the ANZAC Hall.

events.id: Kaicycle Community Open Day Mei (May) - Climate Festival edition!-7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Kaicycle Community Open Day Mei (May) - Climate Festival edition!
events.venue: 7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you been looking for a chance to visit Kaicycle Urban Farm? Come along our Community Open Day in Mei / May, as part of the Climate Festival Pōneke.
9:30am - 12.30pm COMMUNITY WORKING BEE - Registrations required.
Growing local kai in ways that are good for the whenua is climate action. Come and get your hands in the māra, learn some skills, and meet some cool people. Please register at www.kaicycle.org.nz/volunteer
12:30PM - 1:30pm, KAI BREAK
Join us for a shared lunch and a chilled hang out in the māra. Please bring some kai to share if you can.
1:00PM - FARM TOUR
If you haven't been to our urban farm before, this is a great opportunity to find out what we're all about and how our farm and community composting works.
1:30pm - 3.00pm KOHA WORKSHOP: Climate Friendly Composting -
https://www.facebook.com/events/1704614757650028/
Registration required - email community@kaicycle.org.nz to register.
It’s well known that composting is a lot better than throwing food scraps in landfill - reducing greenhouse gas emissions, returning nutrients to the soil, improving soil health and microbiology, sequestering carbon in the soil, and growing more kai. But... composting still produces greenhouse gas emissions. The recipe and the composting method used can hugely affect the amount of greenhouse gases emitted during the decomposition of organic materials, and the quality and biological composition of the resulting compost can have climate implications too.
Join us to learn about a range of composting methods and the climate impact they have, and find out what practical techniques you can use to minimise emissions from composting at home and make the most healthy compost possible.
This is a koha workshop and we ask you to pay what you can afford. If that’s a gold coin that is completely fine! If you can afford more we really appreciate it - we are a charity and rely on donations to be able to carry out our mahi. Koha can be paid in cash or by bank transfer.
Registrations required - email community@kaicycle.org.nz to sign up.

events.id: Card Merchant Wellington - Catan Settlement Event #1 2026-Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Card Merchant Wellington - Catan Settlement Event #1 2026
events.venue: Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Card Merchant is excited to be part of the 2026 Catan Organized Play program here in New Zealand, providing an opportunity for players from all over the country to get involved, learn, and enjoy the game in a structured event setting.
These Catan Settlement Events are designed as an accessible entry point into organized play, giving players a chance to experience tournament-style games ahead of more competitive events later in the year.
Event Details:
Store: Card Merchant Wellington
Dates: 9th May 2026
Time: 11:00 AM
Entry Fee: $20
Event Structure:
Players will engage in multiple rounds to determine overall standings for the event.
Preliminary Round:
Each participant will play three separate games of base set Catan.
Opponents will rotate for each game.
At the conclusion of the preliminary round, players will be ranked based on their overall performance across all games.
Final Rounds:
Depending on player numbers, top-performing players may be split into final tables to determine overall placings.
If player numbers are lower, the event may instead consist solely of multiple Swiss-style rounds, with final standings determined by total points or wins.
This event is designed to balance structure with accessibility, giving players a clear tournament experience while keeping the environment welcoming and easy to engage with.
Prizes:
4x Summer Hex Kits
2x Gamegenic Catan Sleeves
2x Exit: Adventures on Catan
1x Catan Seafarers Duel
Whether you’re new to Catan or a long-time player, this is a great opportunity to get involved, meet other players, and take part in organized play. Join us at Card Merchant for a fun and welcoming day of Catan!

events.id: New Year New Challenge - 1 Million models-64 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: New Year New Challenge - 1 Million models
events.venue: 64 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
STARTING NOW - THIS ENDS 9TH OF MAY!
Help us reach a target of one million miniatures painted in 2026, and Introduce new people to Warhammer to earn rewards!
This year, we've got two great challenges for you - One is about challenging yourself, and the other is aimed at growing the community with friends and family.
Our first challenge is one with a lofty goal – the Million Miniatures Challenge. It’s exactly how it sounds, we want to see one million painted Warhammer miniatures. Taking part is simple: head down to your local Warhammer store from the 17th of January and pledge to paint 25+, 50+, or 100+ miniatures by the 9th of May.
Faction, game system, and size of miniatures doesn’t matter, it’s just how many you can paint. But it’s extra impressive if your numbers include the likes of Fulgrim, Stompas, and Archaon.
Every pledge will be recorded on the store’s hub poster, and you’ll get a bespoke activity card to fill out your commitment and your continuing progress. As you hit your milestones, you’ll be awarded with a series of cool gifts at the Million Miniature celebration event on Saturday 9th May. You’ll get a One Million Miniatures pin badge for 25+ miniatures, a purity seal water pot stand for 50+, and a paint brush tin for 100+*
One million miniatures is a big number to reach, even across every Warhammer store involved. Even with hundreds of prolific painters involved, everyone will need all the help they can get, which is where the second challenge comes in.
Call to Arm's
https://www.facebook.com/share/18D1fD4YY3/

events.id: Climate Friendly Composting-7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Climate Friendly Composting
events.venue: 7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Registrations essential. Please register by emailing community@kaicycle.org.nz
This event is hosted as part of Climate Festival Pōneke 2026 & Composting Awareness Week.
It’s well known that composting is a lot better than throwing food scraps in landfill - reducing greenhouse gas emissions, returning nutrients to the soil, improving soil health and microbiology, sequestering carbon in the soil, and growing more kai. But... composting still produces greenhouse gas emissions. The recipe and the composting method used can hugely affect the amount of greenhouse gases emitted during the decomposition of organic materials, and the quality and biological composition of the resulting compost can have climate implications too.
Join us at Kaicycle Urban Farm to learn about a range of composting methods and the climate impact they have, and find out what practical techniques you can use to minimise emissions from composting at home and make the most healthy compost possible.
Welcome to join for any other part of the day -
9.30am-12.30pm - working bee (see kaicycle.org.nz/volunteer
12.30-1.30pm - shared lunch, bring something to share if you can!
1:00PM - FARM TOUR - If you haven't been to our urban farm before, this is a great opportunity to find out what we're all about and how our farm and community composting works, before the workshop starts.
1:30-3PM - CLIMATE FRIENDLY COMPOSTING WORKSHOP
Admission Fee: Koha

events.id: Two Tunnels - Janes Walk 2026-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Two Tunnels - Janes Walk 2026
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Join AWE awesome walking envisioned in collaboration with Living Streets for the fifth Janes Walk event. The Two Tunnels citizen-led walk will follow the proposed path of a roading project in central Wellington. Questions to ask along the way are what will we lose if the project goes ahead, and what would we prefer in its place.
Jane Jacobs was a well respected urbanist of last century who valued the organic nature of cities and successfully worked to stop motorways through central New York and Toronto.
RSVP WalkingAWE@gmail.com.

events.id: & Juliet-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: & Juliet
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
What might have happened if Shakespeare’s Romeo and Juliet had ended differently? A ‘what if’ scenario is given life in a new musical coming to Wellington in 2026. & Juliet explores the idea of what the Bard’s heroine might have done if she’d lived beyond the end of the play. The bold reimagining uses fantastic songs to tell a story through a fiercely independent lens. The result is a fabulous, feel-good journey of self-discovery, empowerment, and second chances. 
& Juliet is a jukebox musical. That means the story is told using a playlist of pop bangers you already know and love. It features chart-toppers like Since U Been Gone, Roar, Baby One More Time, Larger Than Life, and Can’t Stop the Feeling! The show pulses with energy, heart, and non-stop fun. 
You’ll be wowed with dazzling costumes, electrifying choreography, and hit after hit. The retelling of a classic love story through a bold new lens makes & Juliet more than just a musical. It’s a celebration of rewriting your own narrative.

events.long_description:
Presented by: Your Favourite Band
Your Favourite Band (YFB) is proud to present The Music of Kendrick Lamar: Reimagined Live.
Titans of Aotearoa music celebrating one of hip hop’s most influential voices.
Kendrick Lamar (K-Dot, Kung Fu Kenny, Cornrow Kenny, oklama) is known for pushing boundaries—blending sharp storytelling with jazz, funk, soul, and experimental hip hop, and unquestionably reshaping the sound of modern hip-hop.

Don’t miss out on this homage to one of modern music’s most important voices at Wellington’s own Meow Nui.

events.long_description:
GALA NIGHT $55 includes two events and a grazing table, cash bar
TUSIATA AVIA LIVE
Saturday 6.30pm–7.30pm, Anzac Hall
Award-winning author, international performer, Member of the New Zealand Order of Merit, and subject of numerous moral panics, Tusiata Avia has lived many lives and built a reputation for creating and performing mesmerising, fearless, challenging work. Tusiata will appear at Featherston Booktown for the first time in conversation with RNZ's Susie Ferguson to discuss her incredible journey and to share from her latest book, Giving Birth to My Father, a rich, unflinching collection about learning to live with a loss that seems too heavy to bear.
In partnership with the Creative New Zealand Pasifika Arts Strategy Team.

events.long_description:
Every year, NZGF runs The Pavs as a celebration of ANZ’s best and boldest game development achievements. From digital to tabletop, we aim to the put a spotlight on local games that have gone the extra mile to immerse, entertain and tantalise. events.id: Night Out at the Roxy | Ocean's Eleven-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Night Out at the Roxy | Ocean's Eleven
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A NIGHT OUT AT THE ROXY
You're either in or you're out, gather your team and get ready to take the house in one of the greatest heists! A Night Out at The Roxy invites you to a special screening of Ocean's Eleven (2001). 3 Casinos. 11 Guys. 150 Million dollars. Ready to win big?
Upgrade your experience with our "Rusty's snack box" bundle, paired with a themed cocktail or mocktail and a couple of savory bites.
--- Dapper Danny Ocean is a man of action. Less than 24 hours into his parole from a New Jersey penitentiary, the wry, charismatic thief is already rolling out his next plan. He follows three rules: do not hurt anybody, do not steal from anyone who does not deserve it, and play the game like you have got nothing to lose. With those in mind, Danny works to orchestrate the most sophisticated, elaborate casino heist in history, targeting three establishments owned by the man who is dating his ex-wife. ---
Blame it on the crew - "Ocean's 11 has always been one of my favorites films, as someone who used to work in a casino vault, I can't help but appreciate the detail, the planning, and just how bold the whole operation really is!" -Edhel

Learn the essential sewing skills you need to get you started in this one-day workshop. You'll take away your own lined bag and lots of ideas and inspirations for further projects.

events.name: Cake Decorating Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cale Decorating Workshop
Join cake expert, Lina, for a hands-on cake building and decorating workshop where creativity meets craftsmanship!
With over 20 years of experience in the culinary and hospitality industries - from managing restaurants across Europe and Asia to running her own cake design business - Lina brings not only technical expertise, but also a genuine passion for sharing the joy of decorating.
Known for her friendly, engaging style and eye for detail, Lina will guide you through each step of the process - from building cakes and decorating including palette knife and  gum-paste techniques, to using buttercream and creating a centrepiece.
Whether you're a complete beginner or simply want to sharpen your decorating skills, you'll leave with new techniques, confidence, and of course, your own edible masterpiece.
Let’s make something beautiful - and delicious - together!
You will leave with a tall 6” decorated cake in its own cake box to take home and enjoy. All ingredients included in course fee - just bring an apron and a couple of teatowels. 
Not suitable for those who are gluten-free. 
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Explore how to appropriately structure and polish your CV and cover letter in order to achieve optimum results. This course prioritises you. Identify your key skills and weave these into your CV and cover letter to land you your next job interview.
 Whatever job you are going for, there are key characteristics that should not be missed in your CV and cover letter. The focus of this course is to provide you with the same lens as a recruiter. By learning how your job application is reviewed by a recruiter, you will gain skills to streamline your CV, and improve your cover letter writing. This will increase your chances of being a successful candidate and sets you up to land an interview for your ideal job.
 It doesn’t matter what previous experience you have - everyone has a range of skills that they can offer to their future employer. Fortunately, a strong and realistic CV and cover letter will showcase these skills and increase your chances for job success.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Recognise the features of an effective, well-crafted CV and cover letter and apply these to your own job applications;
- Apply different approaches, styles and techniques while writing your CV and cover letter depending on the context and job;
- Help others with their CV and cover letter writing (friends and whānau).
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings
In this challenging and exciting workshop students will learn to set glimmering round-cut faceted gemstones into brass, copper and/or silver. Create a stone set jewellery project from a choice of simple earrings, a pendant, or a ring.
Students who have already done a Lost Wax Cast Rings class may bring their cast rings and try their hand at setting stones in those instead of starting a new project.
Students will learn ‘Flush Setting’, a method of embedding gems into the surface of metal without the need for solder or joins. The process is adaptable to a number of jewellery types and styles. Students with prior Flush Setting and soldering experience may advance to other methods and to try their hand at learning ‘Bead Setting’, or a simple form of ‘Prong Setting’.
Students may choose from lab-created Sapphires, Rubies and other high strength stones such as Cubic Zirconia in a range of colours.
The workshop is especially satisfying for the magpie in all of us, adding extra colour and shine to jewellery making.
While both beginners and returning students are welcome in the class, be aware that the tools and techniques require a level of fine motor skills, keen eye for detail, and strong hand control which take some patience, practice and perseverance to get the best results. 
Our magnifying headsets, work clamps, and patient tutor will help you along the way. 
Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
By the end of the course, students will be able to:
Develop jewellery making skills with introductory faceted stone setting methods
Create a simple stone set project - either earrings, a pendant, or a ring.
Use Vernier Calipers to measure and calculate metal dimensions
Use files to shape brass, copper and/or silver components
Use a soldering torch to attach metal components such as jump rings
File, sand and polish brass, copper and/or silver creations into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Men’s Health & Aging Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Men’s Health & Aging Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Men's Health & Aging: Take Charge of Your Future
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Aging is inevitable, but how you age is up to you. This essential course offers men a rare opportunity to confront the physical, mental, and social changes that come with age, with a holistic approach to staying strong and engaged.
What We’ll Cover:
Physical Health:
Stay Ahead: Preventive care strategies to keep you at your best.
Fuel & Fitness: Nutrition and exercise tips tailored for men over 40.
Managing Chronic Conditions: Tackle issues like hypertension and diabetes head-on.
Mental Health:
Stay Sharp: Techniques to manage stress, anxiety, and maintain cognitive health.
Mind & Mood: Discover mindfulness and relaxation for a balanced mind.
Movement & Activity:
Keep Moving: Boost flexibility, balance, and strength to stay active.
Strength Training: Simple routines to maintain muscle and vitality.
Your Role in the World:
Purpose & Identity: Redefine your role and find fulfillment in later life.
Community & Connections: Build lasting relationships and a strong support network.
Balance: Master the art of balancing work, life, and leisure.
Fact vs. Fiction:
Media Myths: Challenge aging stereotypes and embrace a positive outlook.
Real Talk: Get the facts about aging and ditch the myths.
Why Join?
This course isn’t offered everywhere, and it’s more important than ever. Take charge of your health, your life, and your future. It’s time to embrace aging with confidence and resilience.
This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.

events.id: Psychology of Money Management-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Psychology of Money Management
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Psychology of Money Management
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Managing finances should be empowering and enjoyable, yet traditional methods often make money a stressful, taboo topic—even with your closest loved ones. In this workshop, we're not here to tell you to cut your budget or skip your favourite treats. Instead, we’ll come together to design a financial plan tailored to your unique needs, making you feel confident, excited, and in control.

In this workshop, you’ll explore the psychology of money and how it influences your financial decisions. You’ll also learn the basic investing concepts that are essential for building wealth, so you can understand them better when making choices about your future. Through real-life case studies, step-by-step guidance, and collaborative exercises, you’ll design a financial system that suits your lifestyle, reduces stress, and helps you achieve your goals.
If you want to simplify your finances and live without the worry of every dollar spent, then this is the workshop for you!
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Disclaimer:  The tutor is not a financial advisor. No investments, products nor specific recommendations will be provided.  A step-by-step planning process will be taught by working through a case study.   Any product names or websites used in examples and explanations to help participants understand financial concepts and the planning process are used for clarity only. They are not given as any type of recommendation.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Retirement Reinvented
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Retirement Reinvented
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.
It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the day, you’ll have:
Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your values and your strengths
Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explore choices that can provide you with that sense of purpose
Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Begun to develop your blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and have considered a “future proofing” downsizing plan.
Bring pen and paper.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Level Up Workshops (Ages 10-18)-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Level Up Workshops (Ages 10-18)
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
As part of the New Zealand Games Festival, we're excited to bring you workshops hosted by people and organisations from our local games industry. You'll discover what games are all about and learn some new skills!
Through our cornerstone Level Up series of educational workshops, we're delighted to introduce you to what we do. This is a great opportunity for people outside of the industry to get a glimpse of game development!
All workshops will be held on Sunday 10th May at the Renouf Foyer, Michael Fowler Centre.
To register for these free workshops, please follow the ticket link to specific workshop on the event page.
LINK TO WORKSHOPS EVENT PAGE
https://nzgamesfest.com/level-up-workshops/


★ Game Design Using Godot (Beginner) ★
— Free workshop, 10AM until 4PM
Hosted by Tūhura Tech
LINK TO BEGINNER WORKSHOP http://sessions.tuhuratech.org.nz/.../019d46a6-a70a-7ef3...
This workshop is aimed for ages 10-18 with no previous programming/game creation knowledge needed. Bring your own laptop if possible, but limited devices will be provided by Tūhura Tech.
In this all-day workshop, beginners will be introduced to the Godot game engine and assisted in creating a simple 2D platformer game. This is like a taster course for game development, using free tools and with plenty of resources available! Participants will be taught the basics of Godot editor, how to create playable characters, movement controls, and creating levels. Participants are welcomed to take their digital project home and keep building their projects!


★ Game Design Using Godot (Advanced)★
— Free workshop, 10AM until 4PM
Hosted by Tūhura Tech
The advanced stream of this workshop is more suitable for ages 10-18 who already have some experience programming and making games. Participants are welcomed to sign up solo or to team up with a friend. The free game engine Godot will be used for this workshop and tutors will be present to provide assistance and to help solve bugs. Participants are welcomed and encouraged to take their projects home and keep creating!

events.id: Natural Textured Rings-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Natural Textured Rings
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Craft a beautiful, personalised ring - a perfect treat for yourself or a thoughtful gift for someone special! This 3-hour taster class is designed for absolute beginners, though all skill levels are welcome to join the fun.
You will be guided step-by-step through the core techniques of silversmithing. You'll start by practicing basic texturing, cutting, and shaping with traditional jewellery tools using copper or brass. Once you’re comfortable, you'll move on to crafting your final piece in sterling silver.
In this hands-on workshop, you will learn to:
Measure your finger and cut the metal strip to the perfect length.
Create unique surface textures using a rolling mill, hammers, punches, and even natural elements like feathers or leaves. You can stamp names, dates, whatever you like.
Work safely with a jeweler's torch for heating and soldering the metal.
Finish your ring with sanding and polishing techniques.
By the end of the class, your beautiful, custom-made sterling silver ring will be ready to wear.
This is an ideal activity for individuals, groups of friends, or visitors to the city looking for a creative experience.
What's Included:
All practice materials (copper and brass).
A strip of sterling silver for your final ring.
All necessary consumables (sandpaper, solder, chemicals).
Use of all tools and equipment.
Important Note for Young Makers: Kids aged 12 and over are welcome to attend, but anyone under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.
Workshop fee is $165, Early bird pricing $148 (ends 27 April).

events.id: Samba Independente-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Samba Independente
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Samba Independente is singer Alda Rezende’s new(ish) samba band. With relatively rare appearances, each performance feels like an event. The group comes together for select occasions, bringing vibrant live samba to stages like the Rogue on May 9th.

Samba is a vast rhythmic umbrella, and Samba Independente embraces its full spectrum: from roots samba and samba rock to traditional grooves, samba-canção, samba paulista, and samba de breque. The result is a rich, dynamic sound—fresh, expressive, and full of character.

When Samba Independente takes the stage, it’s all about infectious rhythm, strong musical interplay, and getting people moving to the unmistakable pulse of samba.

We are: Alda Rezende (vocals), Felicien Houet (guitar) Jeanette Spicer (flute) Julie Bevan (cavaquinho) Adriana Gimenes (percussion) Matheus Peres (percussion), Tomas Souza (Percussion) Nikkie Rich (percussion, vocals) Francis Tcheliski (percussion)

Music from 9:30, FREE ENTRY

events.name: Balboa Foundations Workshops with Lauren and Andrew
events.venue: 13 Vennell St, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for Balington or just brush up on your Balboa basics by joining Sugarfoot Stomp for TWO exciting classes (+ social practice) with very special guest teachers, Lauren and Andrew.
These classes go over Balboa foundations and are for anyone new to Balboa or looking to brush up on the basics. This is an ideal opportunity if you're a 'bal-baby' or simply need a refresher on the fundamentals. Even if you’re not attending the Balington workshops, or even the socials, it’s a great chance to learn from Wellington’s best!
Who Should Attend:
Balington Full Pass holders with no Balboa experience: Attendance is mandatory. We want the workshops to run as smoothly as possible, and it helps if everyone has their basics down.
Balington Full Pass holders with some Balboa experience: Highly encouraged! Even experienced dancers benefit from polishing their basics — you might be surprised what you can improve.
Not attending Balington? All are welcome if you're interested in learning Balboa. If you plan to attend Balington socials, it'll be a great idea to join.
When:
Sunday, 10th of May
Doors Open: 12:45pm
Class 1: 1:00pm-2:15pm
Class 2: 2:30pm-3:45pm
Practice time: 3:45pm-4:15pm
Please attend both Class 1 and Class 2 — the skills from each class build progressively.
Venue:
Vogelmorn Hall, 13 Vennell Street, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021
Class Prices"
SFS Member and/or you have a Full Pass to Balington: $30
Non-SFS Members: $40
Registration via the link below or on the Balington Event page on our website: https://docs.google.com/.../1FAIpQLSeQQNqA.../viewform
About Lauren and Andrew:

events.id: Mumosas and Mugs-264A Cuba St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Mumosas and Mugs
events.venue: 264A Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This one’s for you Mums. Come enjoy an afternoon of Mumosas, clay and some fun with us.
This one’s for you, Mums!
Your ticket includes:
All pottery materials
Guided instructions to build your custom mug
A welcome drink to enjoy while you create

You’ll get around 600g of clay to make your very own mug, with M+W Pottery guiding you every step of the way. You’ll have fun tools to customise your mug to your liking!
After building, you’ll choose one glaze color, which M+W Pottery will apply at our studio, followed by a clear coat finish for a polished, professional look. Your piece will be ready for pickup at Heyday Brewery approximately 10–12 weeks after the event.
Please note: Clay is a natural material, and sometimes pieces can break during firing—this is part of the pottery process. All sales are final; no refunds available 7 days prior to the event.

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks

Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party

Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.
And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!
Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.
1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social
Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.
Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.
Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.
Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

events.id: Stitch and Butch @ Blondini's-10 Kent Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Stitch and Butch @ Blondini's
events.venue: 10 Kent Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Stitch & Butch @ Blondini’s!
A casual meet up of crafters within the rainbow community
New members welcome.
Bring your knitting/crochet/cross stitch/embroidery/crafting/art or just come and have a chat.
Feel free to come partway through the time or leave early as needed.
Stitch and butch is a playful twist on the stitch and b!tch name to reflect the queerness of the group. Attendees do not need to be in any particular subset of the queer community to be welcome.
Usually gathered around one of the tables in the lounge area upstairs. Often near the walls for the brighter lights to better see our stitches.
There is a lift as well as stairs and Courtenay place bus stops across the road.
Held on the 2nd and 4th Mondays of the month with an end of year break.

events.id: Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour-10 Kent Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour
events.venue: 10 Kent Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour presented by the NZ Alpine Club
The New Zealand Alpine Club with Presenting Partners, World Expeditions, La Sportiva, NZ Mountain Safety Council and Osprey, bring the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour back to Tāhuna Queenstown this April! Banff Centre Mountain Film and Book Festival is one of the largest and most prestigious mountain festivals in the world! Hot on the heels of the Festival that is held every fall in beautiful Banff, Alberta, the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour hits the road. With stops planned in over 600 communities and more than 40 countries across the globe, the Banff World Tour celebrates amazing achievements in outdoor storytelling and filmmaking worldwide. From the over 400 entries submitted to the Festival competition each year, award-winners and audience favourites are among the films that are carefully selected to play in theatres around the world. Travelling to remote vistas, analysing topical environmental issues, and bringing audiences up-close and personal with adrenaline-packed action sports, the 2025/2026 World Tour is an exhilarating and provocative exploration of the mountain world.
Be moved. Be inspired. Don’t miss out. Reserve your tickets today. For more information visit banff.nz and follow our Facebook page (LINK) for updates.
This screening is a fundraiser for the New Zealand Alpine Club.
We will be screening two programmes, Ruapehu and Tasman over two nights. More details are available here: https://banff.nz/faqs/

events.id: Analogue Aotearoa w/ Kimberly Cannady — Part of Analogue Aotearoa nights at National Library 2026-Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Analogue Aotearoa w/ Kimberly Cannady — Part of Analogue Aotearoa nights at National Library 2026
events.venue: Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for our Analogue Aotearoa nights, a series of listening parties during New Zealand Music Month that aim to celebrate music from the library’s collections.
Kimberly Cannady will introduce ‘Introducing Dinah Lee’.
Listening parties
May is Te Marama Puoro o Aotearoa New Zealand Music Month, and we are excited to offer a series of three listening parties, featuring records from the National Library’s collections.
The records will be presented by experts who have written the book on the vinyl. Listeners will get to hear an introduction to the music, needle drops on the vinyl itself, and be able to ask questions about the music from the presenters afterwards.
Listen to the record ‘Introducing Dinah Lee’ at the Library
In this second instalment of our adventures on wheels, Kimberly Cannady will introduce Introducing Dinah Lee in our adventures on the wheels of steel.
We look forward to welcoming you. Please enter via the Molesworth Street entrance.
Join us for other events in this series: https://natlib.govt.nz/.../analogue-aotearoa-nights-at...

events.id: Night Photography - In the Dark-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Night Photography - In the Dark
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Night Photography - In the Dark
This course gives you the opportunity to explore and sharpen both your technical and creative skills, so you have the ability and vision to capture the enchanting world that resides in the darkness.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Identify the equipment needed to take photographs at night
• Learn fundamentals of photography composition and editing of night images
• Identify how to use technical photographic tools in a night time context
• Explore techniques that allow you to develop a more creative approach to picture making including: light painting, intentional camera movement and light trails
• Recognise the importance of light and colour in night photography and develop your ability to use it to make dynamic images
Course includes a couple of evening field trips (tutor will advise of suitable dates once course is under way).
You must bring a tripod and, if you have one, a neutral density filter and cable release remote. You need a fully manual DSLR or mirrorless camera and an intermediate understanding of camera functions. Not suitable for point and shoot cameras.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bear's Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bear's Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa. They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May. Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday
Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade. Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically. Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying.
Their emotive story telling and cinematic sound has made up a catalogue that translates into film and onto live stages, taking them to some of the world’s biggest arenas and festivals, including Reading and Leeds, Glastonbury Festival, Pukkelpop, Lowlands and Bonnaroo.
Consisting of founding member Andrew Davie on vocals and guitar, Christof van der Ven (banjo, guitar, vocals), Marcus Hamblett (synths, horns), and Jools Owen (drums, horns), the band have been on the road celebrating the 10 year anniversary of Islands for the past year and they’re now set to bring it all to New Zealand for the first time.

events.id: Morning Commuter Coffee Club at Onepū Cafe-226 Onepu Road, Lyall Bay, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Morning Commuter Coffee Club at Onepū Cafe
events.venue: 226 Onepu Road, Lyall Bay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Another easy, welcoming stop for riders heading through the eastern suburbs. Swing by for coffee, a bite and conversation, and learn more about local support available for getting around by bike.
Whether you’re riding to work, heading to school drop-off, or just out for a morning spin, swing by Onepū Cafe and enjoy your coffee on the beach or outside the cafe.
This is all about connection, supporting local businesses, and making your morning commute something to look forward to.
It’s also a great chance to get involved in Bike Buddies, whether you’re new to riding and keen for a bit of support, or an experienced rider who’d love to help someone else build confidence getting around the city.
Find out more about Bike Buddies Wellington on Facebook or email them at bikebuddieswellington@gmail.com

Tai Chi
Instruction is given in 8 Yang-style forms and you will be able to perform these forms with confidence.  You'll also gain a good understanding of the martial applications and health benefits involved.
By the end of the course, you'll have had an introduction to this martial art and will: 
• Be able to demonstrate the Yang-style forms
• Have enhanced suppleness
• Have better balance
• Have better concentration
• Have practised exercises for enhanced blood circulation and energy
Wear loose, comfortable clothing and bring a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bear’s Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Bear’s Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa. They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May. Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday

Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade. Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically. Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying. 
Their emotive story telling and cinematic sound has made up a catalogue that translates into film and onto live stages, taking them to some of the world’s biggest arenas and festivals, including Reading and Leeds, Glastonbury Festival, Pukkelpop, Lowlands and Bonnaroo. 

Consisting of founding member Andrew Davie on vocals and guitar, Christof van der Ven (banjo, guitar, vocals), Marcus Hamblett (synths, horns), and Jools Owen (drums, horns), the band have been on the road celebrating the 10 year anniversary of Islands for the past year and they’re now set to bring it all to New Zealand for the first time. 


Tues 12 May 
Tuning Fork | Auckland 

Wednesday 13 May 
San Fran | Wellington 

Follow Bear’s Den 

Website | Instagram | Facebook | Spotify | Apple Music | TikTok
