events.id: TThe Happiness Challenge: Stop the World, I Want to Get Off?-FREE EVENT AT VIC-Fuji Xerox Print Shop, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: TThe Happiness Challenge: Stop the World, I Want to Get Off?-FREE EVENT AT VIC
events.venue: Fuji Xerox Print Shop, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Happiness Challenge: Stop the World, I Want to Get Off?
Uni life moves fast — deadlines, pressure, social stuff, parties, trying to keep up.
But is it making you happy?
Experience a session with Devamrita Swami, international motivational speaker, monk, and author of Hiding in Unnatural Happiness. He has spent years speaking at universities worldwide, challenging how we think about success, happiness, and what it’s all for.
This isn’t surface-level motivation. It’s a chance to step back and look at the bigger picture:
Are we chasing the right things? And if not… what’s the alternative?
Drawing insights from his well-known book Hiding in Unnatural Happiness, he’ll unpack why modern life can feel full but still leave you unsatisfied and offer a different way to approach it.
With a bit of live music, real conversation, good food & networking.
Plus fre kai!
Loaded nachos (salsa, taco ‘meat’, sour ‘cream’ & ‘cheese’)
Strawberry & peanut butter milkshakes
brownie
All plant-based!

Click ‘Get Tickets’ to save your seat (and your doughnut).
tinyurl.com/thehappinesschallengeVicUni
Hosted by yoga lounge club.

Australasia’s only festival dedicated to fulldome cinema, the Dome Under Film Festival (DUFF), makes its Aotearoa New Zealand debut at Te Ara Whānui ki te Rangi Space Place.
Space Place’s ‘Best of the Fest’ screenings feature eight films from Museums Victoria’s 2026 festival line-up including the award-winning and highly commended films.
Arohanui to Museums Victoria for making this festival possible.

Each screening includes two or three films. Tickets include access to Space Place galleries and telescope viewings (on a clear night).

events.id: Brynzilian Big Band-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Brynzilian Big Band
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Brynzilian Big Band is a 17-piece powerhouse jazz orchestra founded and led by saxophonist Bryn van Vliet. Based in Wellington, Bryn is a multi-disciplinary artist known for his dynamic career as an instrumentalist (saxophone, woodwinds, vocals), performer, composer, arranger, and educator. As a founding member of TOI and former lead alto saxophonist for the Rodger Fox Big Band, he has earned a reputation as a versatile and sought-after musician.

The Brynzilian Big Band presents fresh, vibrant compositions inspired by iconic Brazilian music styles such as Samba, Bossa Nova, and Forró. Bryn’s writing reflects his deep affection for Brazilian music, while honoring its traditional roots and spirit. Featuring 17 of Wellington’s finest musicians, the Brynzilian Big Band promises a performance that will make you dance, laugh, shout, cry — and maybe even hug your neighbour.

Music from 9pm, $20 on. the door $10 for students

events.long_description:
A fascinating tour into the world of titiwai, New Zealand's glow worms, in Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā. This outdoor night-time tour takes about an hour, and all are welcome to join. If bringing children, we find it most suitable for those aged 7 years and older.
Meet at the Founders’ entrance to Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā on Glenmore Street. Please bring your ticket, a torch, and an umbrella if it’s raining. We'll see you there!
This tour is operated by the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden.
Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy
• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.
• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.
• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.
For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

events.id: Soul In Motion (Wellington)-38 Dixon Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Soul In Motion (Wellington)
events.venue: 38 Dixon Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
SOUL IN MOTION: WELLINGTON
FRIDAY 15th MAY @ AFTERS
9pm-3am
FEATURING:�
NEED FOR MIRRORS�
AUTOMATIC�
SINGR
PEARLY
�D-DOG
HOSTED BY YOUNG GHO$T
If you’re someone who appreciates the deeper and more soulful side of DNB, this one is for you. Ghost Media and Afters are teaming up to present the Pōneke leg of Soul In Motion’s first ever Aotearoa residency.
Expect a high-octane, full spectrum night of future music straight from the heart, headlined by none other than Soul In Motion’s top dog, NEED FOR MIRRORS.
Joe Moses aka NEED FOR MIRRORS is a true legend of the game, one of the genre’s most prolific and experimental artists, a constant presence in the record crates of many.
Presiding over a distinctly angular and confrontational sound, he has worked with some of the genre’s most esteemed labels, including V Recordings, Souped Up, Metalheadz and Thirtyone Recordings. In 2017, he established his own label - Soul In Motion, which over time has established itself of one of the premier labels and club nights in DNB, with over 40 releases from legends such as Jonny L, Bungle, Mark System, Lynx, Javano and more.
Joining Need For Mirrors we have two of SIM’s brightest stars - Ōtautahi-based pair AUTOMATIC and SINGR.
Both of these producers have been working hard producing tracks for Soul In Motion, receiving all sorts of international recognition and popping up in your favourite DJ’s track listings.
This tour represents an opportunity for them to showcase their sound domestically and demonstrate what the underground of Aotearoa drum n bass really sounds like in 2026.
Opening things up, we have the capital’s busiest selector, PEARLY. Fresh off a run touring with the legendary Shapeshifter, Pearly is an expert in deep and soulful drum and bass, and is planning to open up proceedings with a thoughtfully curated mix of liquid and classic 90s dnb.
On closing duties, we’ve called up Wellington’s very own OG, D-DOG, for a masterclass . After skanking out to him open for Grooverider a couple months ago, we were reminded of D-Dog’s supremacy on the decks, and for a night celebrating drum n bass at its truest and most uncompromising, we couldn’t think of anyone better to close.
On microphone duties of course is Young Gho$t. A man of many genres, he makes no secret about his love for drum n bass, and is exalted for a night that truly celebrates the sound in the way that he perceives it.
Capacity is strictly limited for this one, with $30 early birds available from Humanitix. Secure yours now, and prepare for some serious grooving with these sound providers at the capital’s newest club.
events.description:

Ghost Media and Afters are teaming up to present the Pōneke leg of Soul In Motion’s first ever Aotearoa residency.
On closing duties, we’ve called up Wellington’s very own OG, D-DOG, for a masterclass .

LURE RACING FUN DAY!

events.id: Georgia Knight | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Georgia Knight | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way. After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New
Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band. Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges. Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai. 
It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia. Her independently released debut album Beanpole, described by KEXP as "an evocative set of dimly-lit, hypnotic, psych-tinted pop", earned praise from Sharon Van Etten, Arab Strap, and BBC Radio 6 Music. Critics highlighted her "shadowy, seductive trip-hop textures" (KCRW), "extraordinary vocal range and graveside poems" (The 13th Floor), and "goosebumps"-inducing live performances (BBC Radio 6). 
Supporting the album, she was booked for a string of highly coveted festivals including The Great Escape (UK), Golden Plains (AU), Phoenix Central Park (AU), The Others Way (NZ), and Port Noise (NZ). 
Now touring her 2025 album Beanpole, recorded with Andrew ‘Idge’ Hehir, Georgia returns with a fuller, immersive live show, intimate, cinematic, and impossible to look away from.
events.description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way.
After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.
Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges.
Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai.
It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia.

events.id: Forest giants, fragrant herbs and birdsong - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Forest giants, fragrant herbs and birdsong - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
In this 75-minute walk we will get up close to some delightful pygmy pines before, on a gentle slope, passing under and being awed by their towering cousins en route to a wonderful view of Wellington City.
In the Herb Garden we will see and hear a little about the aromatic, culinary and medicinal properties of familiar and not-so-familiar herbs, before descending, possibly accompanied by some birdsong, through a tract of native forest down to Lady Norwood Rose Garden.
Meet at the Founders’ Entrance, Glenmore Street.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Multi-award-winning Kiwi comic, radio presenter and TV personality Hayley Sproull is back in her hometown. She has a brand-new show that’s not safe for on-air. In Sproull On the Prowl, the self-described walking tornado (over)shares about a wild 12 months. It features stifled farts, hall passes, and an exploration of the Indonesian bush with a cameo from Jesus. 
The Toi Whakaari graduate has come a long way since bursting onto the comedy scene in 2012 when she was named Best Newcomer at that year’s International Comedy Festival. She’s made herself a regular on our airwaves since then through her breakfast radio show and appearances on Taskmaster, 7 Days, Have You Been Paying Attention? and The Great Kiwi Bake Off. Make sure you leave the kids at home for what promises to be a wild night.

events.id: Bob McNeill @ Vogelmorn Upstairs with special guest Emily Roughton-115 The Ridgeway, Mornington, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bob McNeill @ Vogelmorn Upstairs with special guest Emily Roughton
events.venue: 115 The Ridgeway, Mornington, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
One of the most popular New Zealand folk artists of the last two decades, multi award-winning songwriter Bob McNeill brings his acclaimed new album TWELVE DREAMS OF THE RIVER to an intimate concert at Vogelmorn Upstairs in Wellington.
McNeill is joined at Vogelmorn Upstairs by longtime collaborator, Auckland-based fiddle player Emily Roughton. A virtuoso violinist from Whangarei, she tours internationally with her band Rough Town and as a soloist for Celtic dance groups. Classically trained, she is infinitely adaptable with her gorgeous stylings and fiery tune playing and is the perfect foil for Bob's lyrical songs.
McNeill's songs have been covered by artists in NZ, Australia, the UK and USA. New Zealand Musician magazine compared his songwriting to Bruce Springsteen and Mark Knopfler; the Dominion Post likened him to Jackson Browne, James Taylor, Luka Bloom and Dougie MacLean. A compelling storyteller, his songs paint vivid pictures of the moments of decision in people's lives. He has three New Zealand Music Awards (Tuis), all for Best Folk Album. Known for his many collaborations with some of New Zealand's finest musicians, this is his first solo tour in a decade and showcases his unique songwriting, celebrated guitar playing and gorgeous live sound.
McNeill tours his new album TWELVE DREAMS OF THE RIVER. Featuring his signature rich songwriting, the album explores themes of life, age and transition, re-imagined as a trip down three iconic rivers - the Manuherikia in the South Island, the Waikato in the North Island, and the Kelvin in his native Scotland. It's McNeill's most intimate work to date, with some of his most beautiful songs.
Don't miss this intimate live show from one of New Zealand's great songwriters.
“One of New Zealand’s most prominent and gifted songwriters... the songs are beautifully written”
AMERICANA UK
“Timeless songs”
FOLKWORLD
“Endearing, and at times haunting songs”
NEW ZEALAND MUSICIAN
events.description:
One of the most popular New Zealand folk artists of the last two decades, multi award-winning songwriter Bob McNeill brings his acclaimed new album TWELVE DREAMS OF THE RIVER to an intimate concert at Vogelmorn Upstairs in Wellington.
Classically trained, she is infinitely adaptable with her gorgeous stylings and fiery tune playing and is the perfect foil for Bob's lyrical songs.
McNeill's songs have been covered by artists in NZ, Australia, the UK and USA.
Known for his many collaborations with some of New Zealand's finest musicians, this is his first solo tour in a decade and showcases his unique songwriting, celebrated guitar playing and gorgeous live sound.
Featuring his signature rich songwriting, the album explores themes of life, age and transition, re-imagined as a trip down three iconic rivers - the Manuherikia in the South Island, the Waikato in the North Island, and the Kelvin in his native Scotland.

events.id: SLOMOSA - Year Of The Horse Tour Wellington w/Sidewinder & Mammuthus-154 Vivian Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: SLOMOSA - Year Of The Horse Tour Wellington w/Sidewinder & Mammuthus
events.venue: 154 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
SLOMOSA ARE COMING TO NEW ZEALAND!

Hailing from the city of Bergen, Norway, Slomosa channel the sprawl of their surroundings through a sweeping signature hybrid of revved-up stoner rock riffage, grungy hooks, and a concentrated punk wallop. As if holding up a sonic mirror image to the landscape, an avalanche of distortion tumbles into valleys of massive melody, teeming with tectonic force. Now, the group—Ben Berdous [vocals, guitar], Marie Moe [vocals, bass], Tor Erik Bye [guitar], and Jard Hole [drums]—perfect this inimitable sound on their aptly titled second full-length offering and MNRK Heavy debut LP, Tundra Rock.
Joining them on the tour are NZ veteran stoner band Sidewinder.
Since forming in late 2020, the band has carved out a formidable presence in Aotearoa’s heavy music scene with their signature blend of riff-heavy psych, swampy delta blues, and southern stoner rock. 2026 will see the release of their third album - Dominion.
In Auckland they are also joined by Tusk
In Taupo by The Bums
And in Wellington by Mammuthus
Links
slomosamusic.com/
sidewinderband.nz/

Every artist plays 30 minute power sets, keeping the energy high and the selections sharp all night.
Session #020 flips the format completely.
No one faces the DJ, instead you’ll be partying behind them while a live feed plays on a large screen, giving a whole new perspective of the set.
Curated by DJ Gracie, featuring: Lineup (A–Z): Akira Bidois Danger Golde.

events.id: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $49 with ID, all others pay $120).
Put in the hard work on this one day Saturday workshop and experience the benefits of self-knowledge. Let the emotional knowledge you gain inform not only how you live your life but also how you feel about living it. You’ll be asking the big questions about not only how you live your life, but also why. Discover the tools and scientifically based information to inform the exploration of your life’s purpose.
Consider your emotional needs and how they relate to personal meaning. You’ll unwrap the concepts of emotional nutrition and be shown how to be guided by your values and start to think of them as a compass directing the journey of your life. To do this you’ll identify your values hierarchy and learn about how this framework can inform how you live your life. Once you can apply a spirit of consistency to your self-identified larger purpose, as well as your needs, then your life’s purpose, emotional needs and core values, will align. It’s quite a transformational day!
Bring pen and paper.  Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch/snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Develop a Life Purpose Statement
Perform an Emotional Needs Self-Assessment
Elicit Your Core Values
Align your goals with your life purpose, emotional needs and core values
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
The ultimate class in practical cheese making!   Learn all you need to know to replicate making NZ style Brie and Queso Fresco at home.  You will be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of Brie. Then, while the Brie is ripening and setting (don't worry, all terminology will be explained!), we will make Queso Fresco together.  
All ingredients are provided as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, a dishcloth, 3 or 4 tea towels, a pen to take notes and a 2 litre container and plastic re-sealable food bags to take your cheese home.
During the workshop, the following topics will also be covered: Milk and where to source it, hygiene and sanitation, equipment required and the origins of the cheeses you are about to make.  You will also receive comprehensive instruction on how to mature your Brie cheese over the next few weeks.
A small break will be provided and you will have the opportunity to purchase equipment and cheesemaking ingredients after the workshop should you wish to replicate the experience at home.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Make Brie and Queso Fresco
- Recall the best milk to use and where to source it from
- Name and identify what equipment and ingredients you need to make Brie and Queso Fresco
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
Love Singapore Hawker food? Come and discover how to recreate some of these dishes from local ingredients with Mei-Fen, who is passionate about Asian food. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.   NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Prepare a range of Singapore “hawker” style dishes which may Kueh Dadar, Mee Goreng and Laksa
• Follow the recipes provided and prepare them with supervision
• Prepare the recipes from local ingredients
• Identify retail outlets which supply the specialty ingredients required 
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Meet the demand for excellence in the job market head on. Learn how to utilise the STARR technique in the way that recruiters use it. That is, to define and seek out the skills and qualities of the best candidate. Master the STARR skills and feel confident in your next job interview.

Familiarise yourself with the skills needed to ace your next interview. Learn the STARR method as an approach to responding to behavioural based questions. Understand how to use concrete examples from your life as proof that you possess the experience and skills for the job you are applying for. You’ll develop the key interview skill of being able to share your accomplishments through real-life examples.

In this course, you are taught how to identify specific example situations that are unique to you. You’ll learn how to confidently talk about the tasks involved and describe how you completed the key task and met its challenges. We’ll also consider the context of jobs you are interviewing for and how to choose examples that best show the desired skills interviewers are seeking.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Develop a higher level of confidence in approaching job interviews.
- Identify the different components of the STARR acronym and apply them to the job interview context.
- Identify what behavioural based questions are and why recruiters use them.
- Successfully answer behavioural based interview questions by applying the STARR technique.
- Develop skills in using real life examples to show transferable skills in the workplace.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from ChatGPT
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools like ChatGPT are everywhere, but how do you get the best out of them? In this hands-on, phone-based workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make ChatGPT a more useful, reliable partner in your work and life. Whether you’ve tried it a few times or use it regularly, you’ll learn how to write better prompts, refine responses, and avoid common pitfalls without needing any technical background.
We’ll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together so you leave feeling confident and ready to put your new skills to use.
Please bring: A phone with the ChatGPT app installed, and enough battery to last the day.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
Use ChatGPT for research, brainstorming, and creative work
Recognise the tool’s limitations (and how to work around them)
Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
Confidently troubleshoot when ChatGPT gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Are you tired of staring at the same old, boring lamps in your home? Do you wish you could add a touch of personality or a burst of colour to your living space? Come along and learn how to make your very own drum-shaped lampshade – a classic style very much on trend at the moment

 Power BI is a powerful and accessible tool for data analysis and dashboarding, widely used across many industries. This beginner friendly course is designed to be practical and hands on, covering subjects such as:

events.id: Zine Making - Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zine Making - Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zine Making - Introduction
In this workshop you will be guided in making a classic cut and fold 8-page zine from a standard A4 sheet of paper - this is a clever design you can easily photocopy and reproduce later! You will be introduced to a range of techniques and approaches such as cut and paste photos and text, collage and drawing on paper.
If you’re keen to make more zines and share them with the world, there's a broad and growing community of Zine-makers and traders, as well as the well-attended annual Wellington Zinefest!
Paper, newspapers, magazines and basic drawing materials will be provided. We recommend you bring along a sketch book and any favourite drawing materials, newspapers, magazines or other interesting paper material to cut up and collage with in class.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Cut and fold a classic 8-page zine format from a single sheet of paper
Brainstorm content ideas for your personalised zine publications
Arrange & compose text and images to fit a classic 8-page zine layout
Combine cut & paste magazine content with hand drawn/ written content
Create a draft zine of your personal interests
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Art of Connection Day Retreat-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Art of Connection Day Retreat
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Reconnect with yourself, improve your communication, and create more ease in your relationships
Come and spend a restorative day on a beautiful rural property in Makara Valley, just 20 minutes from Wellington City (10 minutes from Karori).
This retreat is an invitation to slow down, step away from the noise of everyday life, and reconnect with yourself, with nature, and with a small group of like-minded people.
Understanding your relationship with yourself and how this shapes the way you connect with others. Through guided discussions and self-reflection exercises, you’ll gain insight into self-talk, self-love, your inner and outer worlds, and the ways we mirror what’s happening within us.
We’ll also explore relationships with others, including attachment styles, communication, boundaries, intentions, and simple, practical tools to create more ease, alignment, and connection in your life.
This learning is gently woven into a relaxing and restorative day. You’ll be supported through a guided meditation, cacao, a reflective nature exercise, yin yoga (gentle stretching and reconnecting with your body), a calming sound bath, and time to rest and unwind.
Your day will include:
– 10am -Welcome cacao, introductions, and a guided meditation
– A workshop exploring your relationship with yourself
– A reflective nature walk with guided prompts
– Group sharing and reflection
– Wholesome vegetarian lunch and time to relax (including use of the spa)
– A workshop on relationships and effective communication
– Gentle yin yoga to support integration through the body
– A healing sound bath
– A closing circle to reflect and take your insights forward
Spaces are limited, only 8 spaces available.
About your facilitator:
Briar is an experienced communication coach supporting mums going through family separation with qualifications in conflict coaching she is also a registered Circle of Security Parenting Programme facilitator (a programme helping parents create healthy attachment and connection with thier children). Briar brings a grounded, compassionate approach to supporting people in understanding themselves and their relationships.
Michelle will be running the yin yoga session, a slow-paced, meditative style of yoga characterized by passive floor poses and gentle stretches allowing you to integrate lessons and reconnect with yourself. Michelle is a versatile and seasoned yoga teacher, her classes are a blend of creativity, intuition, and safety - offering a nurturing space for curiosity and deep nourishment.
Investment:
Early bird: $111 (for bookings before 1st May)
Standard price: $155.
Text or call for more information and details on how to find the venue
027 366 3273.
Bring a yoga mat (have a few spare if needed), journal, drink bottle and open heart

Meet at 10am.
Celebrate the 15th edition of The Distinguished Gentleman's Ride in the Wairarapa on Sunday the 17th of May, 2026!
Register to join an event like no other with over 1000 cities around the world riding on the same day in support of men's mental health and prostate cancer research.

All riders must be registered at The Distinguished Gentleman's Ride website to view local ride details, with details are to remain confidential.
For more information please contact Todd on 027 526 7816 or Karina 021 9665 39

events.id: Stacking Rings - Taster class-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Stacking Rings - Taster class
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Create your very own ring stack from copper, brass and sterling silver in this fast, fun and highly rewarding workshop!
Guided by our experienced and supportive tutors, you’ll be introduced to the fundamental skills of jewellery making while creating a small collection of rings to take home and treasure.
During this 3-hour class, you’ll learn how to measure your finger, cut metal to size, and add textures and patterns using hammers and punches. From there, you’ll be shown how to solder your rings together with a jeweller’s torch before finishing them with filing, sanding and polishing techniques. Along the way, our tutors will share tips and tricks, patiently guiding you so you feel confident every step of the way.
By the end of the session, you’ll have crafted a thick sterling silver ring and stack of a copper or brass rings. Two plain round wires to texture up as you like, and a choice of decorative brass pearl-wire, or square wire.
Each ring you make is completely your own, whether you keep them sleek and simple or experiment with texture and pattern. You’ll leave not only with a stylish, wearable ring stack, but also with the joy of having made something truly unique with your own hands.
This class is perfect for beginners, groups of friends, or visitors wanting a memorable creative experience. Many students find jewellery making a wonderfully mindful activity - slowing down, working with their hands and enjoying the calming rhythm of making.
Your class fee includes all materials, plus all consumables such as solder, sandpaper and polishing materials. Extra silver and other metals are available for purchase in the studio if you’d like to expand your collection or upgrade one of your brass / copper wires for a second silver one.
Children aged 12 and up are welcome, though anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
Come along, have fun, and create jewellery you’ll be proud to wear, or a gift for someone special!
Workshop fee $148, early bird $133 till 17 April.

events.id: SLOMOSA - YEAR OF THE HORSE TOUR - W/ SIDEWINDER & MAMMUTHUS-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: SLOMOSA - YEAR OF THE HORSE TOUR - W/ SIDEWINDER & MAMMUTHUS
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
SLOMOSA ARE COMING TO NEW ZEALAND!
Hailing from the city of Bergen, Norway, Slomosa channel the sprawl of their surroundings through a sweeping signature hybrid of revved-up stoner rock riffage, grungy hooks, and a concentrated punk wallop. As if holding up a sonic mirror image to the landscape, an avalanche of distortion tumbles into valleys of massive melody, teeming with tectonic force. Now, the group—Ben Berdous [vocals, guitar], Marie Moe [vocals, bass], Tor Erik Bye [guitar], and Jard Hole [drums]—perfect this inimitable sound on their aptly titled second full-length offering and MNRK Heavy debut LP, Tundra Rock.
Joining them on the tour are NZ veteran stoner band Sidewinder.
Since forming in late 2020, the band has carved out a formidable presence in Aotearoa’s heavy music scene with their signature blend of riff-heavy psych, swampy delta blues, and southern stoner rock. 2026 will see the release of their third album - Dominion.
In Auckland they are also joined by Tusk
In Taupo by The Bums
And in Wellington by Mammuthus
Tickets via UTR

events.id: C-26-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: C-26
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
First gig for C-26 in 2026 at Rogue and Vagabond. Just $10 at the door or online. We will be there from 9:30 pm. Bring your friends and celebrate with some cool salsa tunes. Spread the love and see you there.

C-26 is a sizzling seven piece salsa band from Wellington. They are a great mix of South American and Kiwi professional musicians who bring vibrant and energetic music to the Wellington scene.

Their sound and style is inspired by the salsa classics from Cuba Puerto Rico and Colombia. Featuring the first female Latino salsa singer in Wellington, Anayibi Loboa from Colombia, C-26 is the Salsa purist's band, fully live, with no backing tracks, C-26 produces a sound and feel built from the classical combination of horns, timbales, congas, guiro, trumpet, saxophone, piano and bass that defines the authentic salsa experience.

Music from 9:30pm, tickets from Hummanitix

events.id: The Day the Earth Stood Still-The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Roxy Cinema
events.name: The Day the Earth Stood Still
events.venue: The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Details
Directed by Robert Wise
Starring Michael Rennie, Hugh Marlowe, Patricia Neal
--- When a UFO lands in Washington, D.C., bearing a message for Earth's leaders, all of humanity stands still. Klaatu has come on behalf of alien life who have been watching Cold War-era nuclear proliferation on Earth. But it is Klaatu's soft-spoken robot Gort
that presents a more immediate threat to onlookers. A single mother and her son teach the world about peace and tolerance in this moral fable, ousting the tanks and soldiers that greet the alien's arrival. ---

This is a TEA TIME TALKIE screening where a delicious Devonshire Tea* awaits for upgraded tickets holders (a cup of tea and traditional homemade scones with cream and jam). This is served upstairs in the Grand Lobby at the intermission.

Admit only tickets are available for $15 or you can upgrade to include a Devonshire Tea for $24.

*No dietaries catered for.

Presented in 4K

Schedule:
2:35pm – Cinema doors open
2:45pm - Feature starts
3:30pm - Intermission / Devonshire Tea available for collection upstairs in the Grand Lobby
3:45pm – Feature resumes

events.id: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
THE WATERBOYS make their long-awaited return to New Zealand in May 2026 their first tour in over a decade!
Critically acclaimed as one of the best live bands on the planet, the 2025 band features double keyboard players Brother Paul from Memphis and James Hallawell from Cornwall and is grounded by killer Irish rhythm section Aongus Ralston (bass) and Eamon Ferris (drums).
THE WATERBOYS will be performing tracks from their audacious new album, Life, Death and Dennis Hopper: a bold, cinematic tribute to the maverick American actor and cultural icon, along with material across their storied catalogue. From the early "big music" of The Whole Of The Moon and classic album This Is The Sea through to the roots-inspired Fisherman's Blues era and music from their ambitious post-2015 output.
Mike Scott comments: "The Waterboys live for the experience of playing, of feeling our music evolve and grow with each consecutive concert. Nothing makes us happier than seeing a long list of dates like these. How will the music change? What will happen that's never happened before? How much fun and thrills can we and the audiences have? Australia and New Zealand come and join us!"

events.id: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s a world-renowned guitarist and master entertainer. Tommy Emmanuel, CGP, is bringing his beloved Maton guitar to The Opera House stage in Wellington. He is considered a living legend in the acoustic guitar world. The Australian musical legend has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling technique. The concert promises to be an inspiring journey as Tommy strums and plucks his way through jazz, rock, country and pop music. Fans will be captivated by the diverse musical landscape he creates. 
Since his career began in the 1960s, Tommy has toured with luminaries like Eric Clapton and John Denver. He has won a Grammy Award and performed for a televised audience of more than two billion at the 2000 Sydney Olympics. Collaborations include guitar greats like Les Paul, Mark Knopfler, Joe Walsh, and Richard Thompson. 
Tommy’s hero, legendary guitarist Chet Atkins, bestowed the title of Certified Guitar Player (CGP) on him. He is one of only four players in the world with the title. Tommy Emmanuel CGP continues to push the boundaries of what is possible on the acoustic guitar.

events.id: DONAL GUNNING & THE BUCKS - AUTUMN TOUR-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: DONAL GUNNING & THE BUCKS - AUTUMN TOUR
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
By popular demand Dónal Gunning proudly announces the Autumn ‘Don’t Give a Buck Tour’. Leaving Dublin in November 2022 for New Zealand in pursuit of adventure and a full-time music career, his fans are hungry to see him and his band The Bucks live once again.
Following a breakout 2025, his sophomore EP, Zero Bucks Given was exceedingly well received across the globe—earning international radio play, landing prominent playlists, and drawing high praise from critics. The EP catapulted Dónal’s streams and monthly listeners to an all-time high, showcasing a powerful evolution in his sound and songwriting. The release featured a triumphant 15 date tour of New Zealand.
The Irishman is going from strength to strength in 2026, his debut album set to drop this winter. The Don’t Give a Buck Autumn tour promoting the singles off the upcoming album plus new material.
Dónal will be performing in Whanganui, Taupo, New Plymouth, Levin, Napier, Palmerston North, Wellington & Masterton. Joined by the mighty bucks Stan Shepherd (Guitar), Mason J Williams (Drums) & Jake Bergstrom (Guitar).
Tickets via UTR

events.id: Wednesday | Wellington w/ Mudgoose -San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Wednesday | Wellington w/ Mudgoose
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
When North Carolina’s noise makers Wednesday first visited Aotearoa off the back of their album RAT SAW GOD, audiences were taken by surprise in the best possible way. A 95bFM review called the band’s Whammy show “a sold out countrygaze extravaganza”, and everyone who came left with big hearts and even bigger smiles. Now, with the band’s unbelievable sixth album Bleeds out in the world, it’s high time that Banished Music and Strange News had their fun pals from Asheville down for a repeat visit, with three shows across Wellington, Lyttelton, and Auckland happening this May.

“Rock records don’t come much better than this” - NME ***** 

Bleeds is the album the band are calling “not only the best Wednesday record—it’s also the most Wednesday record”, with all the elements that make the Southern rock magic of this rare group so special. NME put it beautifully in their five-star review of the album, heaping well deserved praise on Wednesday’s “blown-out distortion, weirdo-country slide guitar and serrated melodies”. Fronted and founded by the fierce and fun Karly Hartzman, whose songs are at once confessional and crack-up, Wednesday have built a formidable reputation for being one of the best - and most dynamic - live bands on the circuit. Joining Karly will be the inimitable and animated Xandy Chelmis on lap steel and pedal steel, Alan Miller (drums), Ethan Baechtold (bass, piano).

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.id: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Michael Fowler Centre is set for two visits by the alt-country/folk musician and his band, The Yarra Benders. The tour is called Tā te Manawa — it literally means ‘heart at rest’. This references Marlon’s wish to take a break after a particularly busy and productive few years. 
Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening. The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’. The second delves into repertoire from his first album and bits of everything in between. Marlon explains the reason for the break, “For nearly 20 years I’ve explored both the physical and musical world in the company of incredible musicians, songwriters and friends. The catch with it is — it can be easy to overlook the toll it can take over time, on body and soul.” Fair not though, this is only a hiatus. Marlon describes it as a cheerful hurrah, “a see you soon of sorts.”

The Michael Fowler Centre is set for two visits by the alt-country/folk musician and his band, The Yarra Benders.
The tour is called Tā te Manawa — it literally means ‘heart at rest’.
This references Marlon’s wish to take a break after a particularly busy and productive few years.
Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening.
The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’.

events.id: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for some of the biggest anthems in the rock world. Rock Tenors roar into The Opera House in Wellington for a night of big tunes and big voices. The Anthems Reloaded Tour celebrates key moments in the Kiwi group’s musical journey. 
The five members of Rock Tenors have wide experience. They cross multiple music genres from opera and musical theatre to contemporary rock and thrash metal. They’ve reinvented the standard rock vocal arrangements with harmonic complexity, so audiences will get a dose of skilled musicality as well as nostalgia. You’ll hear The Rock Tenors’ signature renditions of legendary anthems from bands stretching back to the 60s. Hear Queen, The Animals, Bon Jovi, Aerosmith, AC/DC, Black Sabbath, Iron Maiden, White Snake, KISS, Guns N’ Roses, Red Hot Chilli Peppers, U2, and more. 
The touring ensemble of James Adams, Luke Butson, Ben Hayward, Steve Jones, and Justin Wilson are some of New Zealand’s premier vocalists. Since forming in 2022, they have taken the South Island by storm. They have become the must-see act for their vocal mastery and high-voltage rock singing.

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: Strategy, Skills and Self-Awareness
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Equip yourself with the tools and strategies to thrive in today’s competitive job market. This course is designed to help job seekers at all stages across any industry. Whether you're applying for your first job, changing careers, or re-entering the workforce, this course is relevant for everyone because it requires you to self-reflect on your own journey. You’ll learn to reflect deeply on your goals and strengths while building practical skills to stand out from the crowd.
 You’ll leave with a set of practical tools, clear strategies, and a better understanding of what employers want, so you can step into your next opportunity with confidence.
 You’ll develop a tailored job search strategy that works for you. One that has purpose, highlights your transferable skills, sharpens your professional image, and helps you connect authentically with hiring managers. From crafting a digital-ready CV and setting job alerts, to working with recruiters and networking effectively, you’ll gain tools and insights to streamline the process.
 The course also addresses the emotional journey of job hunting. Learn to manage time and energy, stay adaptable, and build resilience as you face the highs and lows of searching for the right opportunity. You’ll explore how emotional intelligence, digital literacy, and critical thinking play essential roles in first impressions, interviews, and long-term career decisions.
 By the end of this course, you’ll be able to:
Identify and articulate your transferable skills.
Apply both emotional and intellectual strategies to mentor and coach yourself from within.
Develop a job search strategy that aligns with your goals.
Build confidence in presenting yourself professionally and authentically.
Develop a confident, authentic approach to your personal branding and professional networking.
Create and manage a job search plan using shortcuts like job alerts, recruitment agents, and focussed applications.
Maintain a polished digital presence and decide whether the use of professional platforms are for you (LinkedIn).
Understand how to navigate the emotional demands of job hunting with self-compassion, motivation, and adaptability.
Build and maintain a network of trusted referees who reflect your work ethic and potential.
 Whether you're starting from scratch or just need a refresh, this course offers a structured, encouraging path forward to help you land your next opportunity.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
Join us for a fun day of creature making! Design and sculpt a one-of-a-kind character in your own style from polymer clays in a full spectrum of colours!

Working with oven-cured clays and wrapped wire armatures, this workshop offers a variety of modelling methods and approaches - a great opportunity to turn sketches & ideas into tangible 3D forms. Modern clay inventions mean we can create translucent, glittery, semi-flexible, UV reactive or even glow-in-the-dark elements to add more realism or a touch of the surreal into each creation!

Students will be coached through designing and creating a small-scale creature sculpture, beginning simple, and building to a more complex final form as we get familiar with materials.

The class offers beginner students a fun introduction to sculpting organic forms from scratch, and for those with some hands-on creative experience, it offers a great opportunity to experiment, hone and advance your making skills to new levels.

If you can, bring along some design ideas, sketches or reference images to help kick start the sculpting process on the day.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
An interactive workshop presented by experienced physiotherapist Sara Dore, focusing on how movement and function can be challenging for women as they age. Particularly for women, there is more to the core than abdominal muscles and strength and we explore these broader aspects.  Most importantly, you will come to understand that movement is medicine and we can make a difference.
You will be able to make a positive difference for yourself! The objective of this workshop is to give you knowledge and understanding about how you can manage the effects on your health and wellbeing as we age as women.  Knowledge is power!
In this one-day workshop you will discover:
- The base of the pelvic floor. Anatomy of the pelvic floor, and the common conditions urinary incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse will be discussed. Education on basic pelvic floor advice and management as well as appropriate exercises will also be covered.
- The middle deep abdominal muscles. Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed. The principles of Clinical Pilates versus traditional Pilates will be presented together with the key principles of movement is medicine.
-The importance of the diaphragm and breathing well will also be discussed to enhance optimal movement and function for women.
Please wear comfortable clothes and socks as this is an interactive workshop, and bring a yoga mat or beach towel.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed.

events.name: Writing Short Stories
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Writing Short Stories
In this one-day course, we’ll explore the elements that make short stories shine, from creating compelling character dynamics, to choosing the point of view that best serves your story.
Through a mix of discussions and practical writing exercises, participants will leave the course with all the tools they need to write engaging short stories.
Please bring pen and paper, and the name of a short story you like.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring your lunch and snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify elements that make short stories memorable
- Create compelling characters and relationship dynamics
- Write using different points of view
- Explore using symbolism in short fiction
- Use detail to develop setting and character traits
- Identify places to send your completed short stories
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Eddie Izzard — The Remix: The First 35 Years-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Eddie Izzard — The Remix: The First 35 Years
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Eddie’s 35 years of sell-out comedy shows stretch from ‘The Ambassadors’ in 1993 to ‘Wunderbar’ in 2019.
Ever wondered what became of Darth Vader and Mr Stevens or the monkey in the tree, did the pears ever ripen… and will God ever appear? It’s a glorious chance to ‘maybe’ find out the answers or discover more questions to those Izzard conundrums. It’s highly likely that no night will be exactly the same, so as ever with Eddie, expect the unexpected.
“In the first 35 years of my stand-up career, I came up with many weird and crazy comedy stories. The ones I like the best will be in my 2025 live Remix Tour,“ says Eddie.
This show promises to be a beautiful, hilarious journey spanning a 35-year career of thought-provoking, intelligent and surreal ideas. Who could resist?
This show may contain strong and imaginative language.

Details
Directed by Tim Burton

30th Anniversary

--- A fleet of Martian spacecraft surrounds the world's major cities and all of humanity waits to see if the extraterrestrial visitors have, as they claim, "come in peace." U.S. President James Dale (Jack Nicholson) receives assurance from science professor

events.id: Wellness Made Simple Masterclass-175 Vivian Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Wellness Made Simple Masterclass
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ready to boost your energy, get better sleep, improve your digestion, rebalance your hormones, and simplify your approach to foundational wellness? Join expert practitioners from The Wellness Collective: Buffy Gill (Nutritionist & Naturopath) and Freda Wells (BSc. Psychology, Yoga Teacher and Somatic Therapist in training) for a powerful, practical masterclass.
In a world where "healthy living" can feel overwhelming, we are bringing it back to basics. Because modern lifestyles and depleted soils mean our food often lacks essential nutrients, this session focuses on using natural whole-food supplements and plant medicine to support your body at a cellular level—restoring balance exactly as nature intended.
In this masterclass, you’ll learn how to:
Boost your energy and improve daily recovery
Get better sleep and build resilience to stress
Improve your digestion and support natural detox pathways
Rebalance your hormones and manage inflammation
Support your health at a cellular level from the foundations up
Whether you’re at the beginning of your health journey and feeling run down, or you're a seasoned natural health lover who wants a simpler, more affordable, and effective regime, this session will help you level up your health across the board with a few simple, consistent daily practices.

events.id: Ockham Awards Author Panel: Tracy Farr and Duncan Sarkies-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ockham Awards Author Panel: Tracy Farr and Duncan Sarkies
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Ockham New Zealand Book Awards celebrate and highlight some of our country’s brightest literary talents. On the 1st of May we will be hosting a very special event with two of the longlisted authors, Tracy Farr and Duncan Sarkies.
This panel will be authors asking authors the big, deep important questions about their work, practice and creative life, providing a unique, intimate, and in-depth insight into the creative processes behind their award nominated works.
Where: Ngā Pou Ruahine space, Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library
This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited; please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our authors:
Tracy Farr
Tracy’s debut novel, The Life and Loves of Lena Gaunt was long­listed for Australia’s Miles Franklin Literary Award, shortlisted for the Barbara Jefferis Award and Western Australian Premier’s Book Awards. Wonderland is her third novel and is set in and during the time of Miramar’s Wonderland.
Duncan Sarkies
Duncan is a writer of novels, theatre and screen stories for film and television. He has been recognised with the Best First Book of Fiction Award at the New Zealand Book Awards for Stray Thoughts and Nosebleeds, and the Bruce Mason Playwriting Award. Duncan’s screenplays include Scarfies, and the adaptation of his first novel, Two Little Boys. His television work includes writing for Flight of the Conchords and What We Do in the Shadows. Star Gazers is his third novel.

events.id: Tension in Te Matapihi - Mystery in the Library-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Tension in Te Matapihi - Mystery in the Library
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Are you a fan of great storytelling? The Ngaio Marsh Awards, in association with Wellington City Libraries, invites booklovers to a fascinating and fun evening of criminally good conversation at Te Matapihi Central Library featuring terrific Kiwi writers.
Two-time Ngaio Marsh Award winning author Jennifer Lane will chair fellow Ngaio winners Brannavan Gnanalingam (Best Novel for SPRIGS), Claire Baylis (Best First Novel for DICE), and 3x winner Michael Bennett, along with Verb Festival founder and kids' author Claire Mabey, and debut novelist Jennifer Trevelyan for a thrilling kōrero about how they craft memorable characters and page-turning storylines, and infuse their tales with deep insights into people and society. We're excited to whisper there has been a very special last-minute guest added to the panel.
WHEN: Thursday 30 April 2026
WHERE: Te Matapihi Ki Te Ao Nui | Central Library, 65 Victoria Street, Te Aro, Wellington
WHEN: 5.30pm
This is a free event.
Brannavan is a Wellington novelist, freelance writer, and property lawyer who was born in Sri Lanka and grew up in Lower Hutt. He's written eight novels, been listed for the Ockham New Zealand Book Awards three times, and won the 2021 Ngaio Marsh Award for Best Novel for SPRIGS, which examined the aftermath of a brutal act of sexual violence. His latest novel is political satire THE LIFE AND OPINIONS OF KARTIK POPAT, published in late 2024.
Claire Mabey is a children's writer, books editor for The Spinoff, and founder of literary festival Verb Wellington. In 2020 Mabey received a Ghost Light Award at the Wellington Theatre Awards, and has been described as "a force of nature in the Wellington arts community". Her middle-grade novel THE RAVEN'S EYE RUNAWAYS won Best First Book at the 2025 NZ Book Awards for Children and Young Adults, among other accolades. The sequel, THE RAVEN'S EYE REBELLION will be published in April 2026.
Jennifer T has a background in photography and children's publishing, and is now a full-time writer living in Wellington, with her husband, son, daughter, dog and cat. When not at her writing desk Jennifer can be found in the garden. Her debut novel A BEAUTIFUL FAMILY became part of a bidding war by global publishers, and was published internationally in 2025.
Claire Baylis is a Rotorua author and legal researcher who won 2024 Ngaio Marsh Award for Best First Novel for DICE, a courtroom drama written as part of her PhD in creative writing from the Institute of Modern Letters. Claire studied then lectured in law for 12 years before moving to Rotorua, where she's been a researcher for the Trans-Tasman Jury Study and set up a water safety programme focused on low-decile schools.
Michael is an award-winning filmmaker and novelist (Ngati Pikiao, Ngati Whakaue) who has won three Ngaio Marsh Awards, across nonfiction and fiction categories - for his true crime work IN DARK PLACES (2017 Best Non-Fiction), detailing the story of Teina Pora, as well as his first two novels starring Māori sleuth Hana Westerman, BETTER THE BLOOD (2023 Best First Novel) and CARVED IN BLOOD (2025 Best Novel). He is co-writer of Irish-NZ crime drama The Gone, and his crime novels have been translated into several languages, been shortlisted for the Acorn Prize for Fiction and several other awards on four continents.
Jennifer Trevelyan is a Wellington short story writer and novelist who was born in rural Australia but has now spent more of her life in Aotearoa. Her debut ALL OUR SECRETS won a Ngaio Marsh Award for Best First Novel, and Jennifer also contributed to crime and thriller anthology DARK DEEDS DOWN UNDER 2. Her novel MIRACLE won the 2024 Ngaio Marsh Award for Best Kids/YA.

events.id: The Devil Wears Prada 2 : Movie fundraiser-29 Wigan St, Wellington, NZ, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: The Devil Wears Prada 2 : Movie fundraiser
events.venue: 29 Wigan St, Wellington, NZ, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for a special fundraising screening of The Devil Wears Prada 2 in support of Rainbow Wellington. This community movie night is a chance to glam up (if you want to) come together, enjoy a great film, and support the important work being done to strengthen and celebrate Rainbow communities in Wellington.
Set a decade after the original movie, TDWP2 revisits the OG cast and some great outfits with Miranda Priestly struggles against Emily Charlton, her former assistant turned rival executive, as they compete for advertising revenue amid declining print media, while Miranda nears retirement.
Very limited tickets are available.
All proceeds from the event will go towards supporting Rainbow Wellington’s work advocating for and connecting LGBTQIA+ communities across the region.
Bring your friends, enjoy a night at the movies, and help make a difference in our community.

events.long_description:
Join Unions Wellington and Reception (the new organising space in Trades Hall) to celebrate International Workers Day (“May Day”) with a pub quiz!
Hosted by comedian and union organiser Tim Keats, this May Day event is sure to rival the heady summer of ‘68.
Drinks, dinner and new merch available!
Win swagged out union prizes by aceing the quiz or through sheer luck in one of our raffles!
Proceeds will be split between Unions Wellington (to run their general election and Wellington Works campaigns) and Reception (to keep the lights on).
Ticket info:
Snacks will be provided on quiz tables.
You can purchase a ticket that includes dinner (if you do not preorder dinner, you may be able to have leftovers, but we will not be doing dinner tickets on the night)
Tickets can be individually purchased, or you can buy a team ticket, max team size is 8 people.
If you don’t have a team of 8 - you can still quiz as a smaller team OR we can add other team members to your team on the day for you.
If you are an individual - we will match you with an existing team, and/or support you to create an alliance with other individuals.
Drinks, Merch and Raffle tickets will be sold separately at the event!
When: 6:00PM Friday, 1 May 2026
Where: City of Wellington Pipe Band Hall, corner Stoke/Hanson St, Newtown
Who: Anyone who wants to support two great union causes.

events.id: May Full Moon Drumming-10A Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: May Full Moon Drumming
events.venue: 10A Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please remember this is a ZERO WASTE and *NO ALCOHOL* community gathering.
NOTE: Due to the recent environmental disaster at Moa Point Waste Treatment Plant, in collaboration with the local WCC ranger we've decided to relocate the event to Herbies Beach in Miramar Peninsula, which is just around the corner past Scorching Bay.
Kia ora!
Full Moon Drumming Wellington is a registered public event with the Wellington City Council and its been running for over 10 years. We are a group of humans from all walks of life who gather under the full moon each month to connect through drumming and dance around the fire. This gathering is co-creative and participatory, that means if you made it to the circle you are part of the circle. There is no audience, everyone is invited to participate as part of the ‘chorus’ (singing/clapping), dancing, or drumming.
The event is weather permitting, we check the forecast a few days prior and aim to make a decision the day before the event, although this is not possible and sometimes we make the decision on the day. All updates are posted on the event page.
This gathering is entirely run by volunteers, if you'd like to contribute in any way please get in touch.
There are a few things that we need help with:
- FIREWOOD: Please bring dry firewood as a koha, no wood no fire! Please NOTE we DO NOT burn pallets (due to nails) or treated timber. Feel free to post or reach out if you have a vehicle and are keen to help gather firewood on the day of the event.
- BEAUTIFYING THE CIRCLE SPACE: Anyone has fairy lights, oil burners, or is keen to make some signs?
NOTE: We hold a fire permit for this event. For safety reasons we don't allow participants to feed the fire, and while is generally okay to roast some marshmallows early in the evening we don't allow the use the fire for cooking or heating food.
This is an **ALCOHOL FREE EVENT**, please help us uphold this kaupapa and remind those who are unaware and might be new to the event that alcohol is not permitted. As hosts we are responsible for your safety and we take that responsibility seriously, especially given that the event is authorised through both fire and event permits. Large, public, all ages gatherings require administration and logistics so please respect this and the guidelines provided.
Children are welcome at the fire, though, especially after dark, please make sure that all children have adult supervision, that the supervisors are aware of the children they are looking after, and that the children are aware of who is looking after them.
Feel free to bring:
- Your friends.
- Drums or any percussive instrument.
- Warm clothing; jackets and wool blankets are good.
- Something to seat on (otherwise sand is often wet)
- Drinking water.
- Food, tea to share.
- Your lovely selves.
- Fire spinning kit and fuel.
FIRE SPINNERS: Please take all precautionary safety measures and make sure to set up fire-spinning downwind from the fire circle.
CARPOOLING: If you don't have a way to get to Princess Bay, or if you have spare seats in your car, feel free to post it on the event page. The less cars driving the better.
Feel free to share the event page and send an event invite to whomever you feel would be interested, not everyone sees the notifications and advertising

Symposium Brass members have performed extensively with all of New Zealand’s professional orchestras and with artists and bands ranging from James Morrison and Diana Krall to The Black Seeds and Salmonella Dub.

Their programme will bring together the best of the orchestral and jazz worlds, including the world premiere of "Progression" by Symposium Brass trumpeter and composer Cameron Pearce, which pulls colours from composers Aaron Copland, Steve Reich, and Gabriel Fauré along with trumpeters Kenny Wheeler and Ron Miles, and new material composed by Pearce for this tour.

events.name: 1/3 Octave Band | Bridget Kelly & Peter Liley | Grant/Winterwood
events.venue: Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
1/3 Octave Band is the ambient/noise/drone project of Jules Desmond (Black Boned Angel) and Bill Wood (Birdcatcher) who conjure slow moving walls of eerie and epic electric guitar. Active since the late 90s, they've released a huge discography on labels like Celebrate PSI Phenomenon and Pseudo Arcana.
www.thirdoctaveband.bandcamp.com
--
The improvising duo of Bridget Kelly (reeds, harp) & Peter Liley (saxophone & electronics) will combine their unique creative voices and impeccable musicality moving from lyrical melodies to unearthly growls, creating meditative yet visceral compositions in real time.
---
Grant / Winterwood: Erika Grant and Zac Winterwood join forces for a performance utilising hammer dulcimer, shruti box, 12 stringed acoustic guitar, and other various instruments.
Erika Grant is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 30+ years musical experience in bands including Orchestra of Spheres, and Cookie Brooklyn and the Crumbs. Over the past few years Erika has developed a sound bathing practice using gongs, bowls and chimes. She is interested in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing.
Zac Winterwood (NZ/AUS) is a musical outlier whose art lives within the tradition/discipline of minimalism. Since the late 1990s, he has been a purveyor in the art of noise, ambient, drone, field recording and experimental music, whose work has been compared to Low, Terry Riley, Brian Eno and Neil Young.
https://www.rnz.co.nz/.../wash-the-mind-with-a-gong-bath
https://winterwood.bandcamp.com/
____________________________________________________

events.id: Cheep Frills (Live) x Rare Specimens-172 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Cheep Frills (Live) x Rare Specimens
events.venue: 172 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Born on East London’s dancefloors and forged across Aotearoa New Zealand’s club and festival circuit, Tempo Schmempo finally lands in Te Whanganui-a-Tara for the first time, with a vibrant series of parties for the winter months.
On a bi-monthly basis, expect carefully curated lineups of the country’s finest underground electronic artists, designed to showcase the fantastic talent that thrives across the motu.
What’s more, each party will be punctuated by a double A-side release from the two artists performing on the night - to be released a week before the party.
And to top it off, we've teamed up with Duncan’s Brewing Co. to pour an official Tempo Schmempo beer on tap for the night.
For the first instalment, we’re proud to introduce Cheep Frills (Live) and Rare Specimens.
About the artists:
Cheep Frills is a genre-bending live act from Marcos Alonso and Dolphin Therapy, fusing electronic production with vibrant vocals and playful performance. Their shows deliver high-energy dancefloor stompers, cheeky reinterpretations, and unexpected sonic detours that keep crowds moving and grinning. Equal parts musically tight and joyfully loose, Cheep Frills is a guaranteed mood-lifter for any stage or setting.
As Rare Specimens, Nick Munday, Pete Green, Matt McPhee and Dan Rainey have become firm fixtures in New Zealand’s underground electronic scene, balancing playful eclecticism with dancefloor precision during their unique back to back performances. United by their love of disco, italo, acid house, Detroit techno, Balearic sounds and UK club culture, the group combines road-tested experience with finely tuned taste acquired through decades of crate-digging and DJing across the globe.
Expect only the finest musical selections, lovingly curated from dance music’s rich history — plus more shenanigans than you can shake a stick at.
See you on the dance floor.

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sat 2 May | Michael Fowler Centre-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sat 2 May | Michael Fowler Centre
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
SOLD OUT! Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.
The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’
Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.
‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’
