events.id: Deafheaven & Nothing-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Deafheaven & Nothing
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Deafhaven return to NZ with special guests Nothing
American black-metal trailblazers Deafhaven announce their return to New Zealand this July, presented by Banished Music. The band will perform two exclusive headline shows in Auckland and Wellington, joined by special guests Nothing.
Formed in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2010, Deafhaven have spent the past decade and a half pushing at the emotional and sonic boundaries of modern metal. From their debut Roads to Judah, to the genre-defining breakthrough Sunbather, and subsequent records like New Bermuda, Ordinary Corrupt Human Love, and Infinite Granite, Deafhaven have continually redefined the limits of heavy music while confronting themes of alienation, addiction, family history, and self-perseverance.
The tour follows the release of their 2025 album Lonely People With Power. Their sixth album and first for Roadrunner Records arrives as both a culmination and a reckoning , an album that embraces the band’s full identity after years of evolution. Produced by Justin Meldal-Johnsen (Beck collaborator and producer of Infinite Granite), the record captures Deafhaven’s innate ability to merge melody with brutality, and pain with poetry. Through reflections on masculinity, inherited trauma, family, and self-perception, the album grapples with the idea that while we can’t fully escape our past or our nature, we can choose how we move forward. Ultimately, Lonely People With Power stands as one of Deafhaven’s most expansive and human statements.
The album has drawn praise from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Line Of Best Fit, Kerrang and more, and features collaborations with Interpol’s Paul Banks and Boy Harsher’s Jae Matthews.
Revered for their transcendental live shows, Deafhaven have toured the world over, performing at festivals such as Coachella and Primavera Sound, and sharing stages with artists ranging from Slipknot and Knocked Loose to Chelsea Wolfe and Mono. They’ve received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Metal Performance and continue to command a reputation as one of the most compelling live acts in heavy music.
Joining them are American shoegaze outfit Nothing, whose latest release A Short History of Decay has been described by Pitchfork as “fresh and exciting.” Their fifth album and first for Run For Cover Records further expands the band’s sonic scope, delivering the most refined and immersive iteration of Nothing to date.

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

Ultimately, Lonely People With Power stands as one of Deafhaven’s most expansive and human statements.

events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music
Following the critically acclaimed release of Asphodels in 2025, local heroes The Veils return with a bold and invigorated new album, Fragile World. Released June 19 on V2 Records, the album marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band, with fearless leader Finn Andrews reflecting on his tendency to make each album as a rebellion against his last. Recorded live to tape at Roundhead by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills), Fragile World captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode. With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, Fragile World stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

Released June 19 on V2 Records, the album marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band, with fearless leader Finn Andrews reflecting on his tendency to make each album as a rebellion against his last.
Recorded live to tape at Roundhead by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills), Fragile World captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode.
With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, Fragile World stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

events.id: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
NZ Citizens and residents pay $219 with ID.  f you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $499 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register..
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.
You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Mental health challenges covered in the course include:
Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:
Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.
If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jeremy Loops tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Jeremy Loops tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: TEG MJR & TEG Live
Get ready as South African sensation Jeremy Loops returns to bring the vibes to Wellington’s Meow Nui this July!
After a massive 2025, including a standout set on the Acoustic Stage at Glastonbury, the world’s favourite high-energy-folk powerhouse is back for a strictly limited run of headline shows.
Known for his legendary live performances that blend acoustic folk, alt pop and urban grit with loop-pedal wizardry, Jeremy Loops delivers a show that is part intimate storytelling, and part stadium-sized party.
From massive breakout anthems like Down South and Waves to the soaring new melodies of his latest record Feathers And Stone, expect an infectious atmosphere and world-class musicianship. Every room becomes a massive communal celebration. Every night is a pure explosion of energy.
Catch his exclusive Wellington performance. One night only, at Meow Nui, supported by Tom River.

events.id: Peter Hook and The Light-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Peter Hook and The Light
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Bassist Peter Hook has intimate knowledge of the music of bands Joy Division and New Order. He’s one of the founding members of both British new wave bands. In July 2026, he is bringing his band The Light to The Opera House in Wellington. Together they will perform New Order’s album ‘Get Ready’ in full to celebrate its 25th anniversary. To make the concert even better, they will also perform a selection of greatest hits from Joy Division’s and New Order’s back catalogue. Get ready to enjoy songs like Disorder, Shadowplay, Transmission, Love Will Tear Us Apart, Blue Monday, and True Faith. 
The dark and ethereal sound of Joy Division and New Order ushered in the post-punk era and continue to be influential in the rock music scene. Peter Hook and The Light are careful to meticulously recreate their music in a live setting. Audiences are in for a show where treasured songs are played with the passion and energy they deserve.

To make the concert even better, they will also perform a selection of greatest hits from Joy Division’s and New Order’s back catalogue.
Peter Hook and The Light are careful to meticulously recreate their music in a live setting.

The Jungle Giants are back, baby!
After touring the world for the past 5 years the energetic 4 piece return to stages down under with with their 5th studio album, ‘Experiencing Feelings of Joy’. Supported by up and comers The Tullamarines and Tear Drive these shows are for tastemakers and dancefloor demons alike.
The album’s lead single ‘Tell Me How it Feels’ is a clear indication of this new era. Fresh vulnerability and sound but distinctly Jungles. With close to half a billion streams under their belt and a reputation for lighting up festival stages this tour unveils a bold new evolution of The Jungle Giants’ signature sound.
Ticket options include: general admission or general admission with a digital download of the brand new album, 'Experiencing Feelings of Joy', which will be emailed on release day, May 8.

events.id: All Blacks vs Italy-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: All Blacks vs Italy
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make it your mission to support the All Blacks in 2026 when they face Italy at Hnry Stadium. This is an exciting match as it’s Italy’s first test in Wellington. It’s also only the fourth time they’ve met in New Zealand since the 1987 Rugby World Cup. With a 5:10pm kick-off, the match is designed to be family-friendly. Bring the whole whanau for an action-packed afternoon at the stadium. 
The All Blacks’ fixture against Italy is part of the inaugural Nations Championship. This is a new international rugby tournament featuring 12 of the world’s top nations across July and November. Each match has competition points on the line leading to the final, so the Wellington game is important. Wellington will be awash with rugby fever, so head out, support the All Blacks and say ‘ciao’ to all the Italian fans in the city.

events.id: Luude-Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Luude
events.venue: Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Christian Benson is known professionally as Luude. The Australian drum and bass producer is best known for his 2021 cover of the 1980s iconic anthem Down Under. The D&B rework gave it a whole new dynamic and sold millions of copies. Five years and many successful mixes later, Luude is riding a wave of success. He’s toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music and now comes to Wellington’s Shed 6. Along with Down Under, you’ll hear big hits like Big City Life, TMO, and Oh My. 
Luude’s recording achievements also include an ARIA Award for Best Dance Release and a nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award. He also won Best International Artist at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, three BBC Radio 1 Hottest Records, and platinum, gold, and silver Certifications. He is also half of Australian electronic duo Choomba.

events.id: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The world’s foremost purveyors of time-twisting hits, Postmodern Jukebox, return to Wellington for one highly anticipated show at The Opera House. 
Founded in New York, the trailblazing troupe made up of singers, dancers, and instrumentalists brings their brand new ‘The Future is Vintage’ tour to New Zealand shores. Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks are reimagined. Expect everything from 70s rock classics and 80s Britpop hits to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. Dress in your vintage best for the full time-travel experience. 
“We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. 
Come and be swept away by the romance and swagger of the past while enjoying your favourite tunes from now.

events.id: Luude | New Zealand Tour 2026-Shed 6, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Luude | New Zealand Tour 2026
events.venue: Shed 6, Wellington
events.long_description:
Age Restriction: 16+ | Doors 7:30PM
Australian producer LUUDE is in the middle of an exciting global breakout moment. Arriving on the scene with back to back global hits "Down Under" and "Big City Life" (which he made for a bit of fun) which have sold millions of copies and earned multi-platinum certifications around the world - he followed this with the Bru-C collaboration "TMO" (UK Top 50) and the Moby sampling "Oh MY" (UK Top 100) - This run of singles makes him one of the highest selling artists in Drum and Bass across the last decade. 
In the short time since he's toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music launched his new imprint "No Instruction" as a much needed outlet for more underground pursuits and built that brand over the last 6 months as a platform for new talent.
Luude's recording achievements also include an ARIA award for Best Dance Release, nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award, "Best International Artist" at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, 3x BBC Radio 1 Hottest 100 Records and 9x Platinum, 2x Gold, 1x Silver Certifications. 


Live Nation VIP Package Terms and Conditions:
- Please note that the contact details provided at the point of purchase, email & mobile, are the details used to communicate all VIP information
- An email will be sent to the original purchaser of the tickets 2-4 business days before the event with timings and additional information.
Please contact Live Nation here if this is not received.
- A text will also be sent with the check in time & location the day before the show. Keep an eye out for a text on the day of the show in case of any changes or additional information.
Note if attending a show in Australia we are only able to send a text to Australian numbers. If attending a New Zealand show, we are only able to send a text to New Zealand numbers. At this time, we are unable to send to international numbers, outside of Australia & New Zealand.
Please contact us here if you have not received VIP Information.
- It is your responsibility as the purchaser to follow up information if it is not received
- VIP purchasers may need to arrive 1-3 hours before doors open to ensure delivery of all benefits
- Original purchasers name must be known in order to check-in and receive your benefits
- If a VIP Gift (e.g. laminate, drink voucher or merchandise item) is included in the VIP package you purchased, unless otherwise notified, VIP gifts will only be available for collection pre-show from the VIP check-in at the venue. VIP Gifts will not be dispatched after the show
- Given the dynamic nature of live events, we may need to make changes to parts of your VIP Package including seating and entry arrangements, start times, merchandise, food and beverage service arrangements and other things. When changes need to be made, where possible, we will endeavour to provide alternatives that overall have an equivalent value as your package
- If applicable, Early Entry refers to first access onto the floor, there is no sectioned off area (unless otherwise stated)
- If applicable, collectible laminate is commemorative only and does not gain or authorise access into the venue or backstage areas
- Choose carefully as we will not provide refunds for a change of mind or because you can’t make the event. Refunds and exchanges will only be made subject to ticketing terms and conditions, and applicable law, including the Australian or New Zealand Consumer Law
- For any questions regarding your concert ticket, please reach out to the point of purchase
- For any enquiries about your VIP Experience check out our FAQ’s or submit a request here

events.long_description:
The year 2026 will see the American exhibition basketball team, the Harlem Globetrotters, celebrate their 100-year anniversary. Wellington gets to celebrate with them when they visit in July. The team will take over TSB Arena and again revel in 100 years of thrills, laughs, and sheer basketball joy. If you aren’t already a fan, then sit back and watch their combined athleticism, sense of theatre, and comedy play out on the court. The gravity-defying dunks and dexterous tricks will have you roaring for more. 
The team has travelled the world visiting more than 120 countries and territories. Their 26,000 exhibition games continue to win new fans wherever they go. From the epic pre-game magic to unforgettable post-game interactions — come and experience their most legendary tour yet.

events.long_description:
Presented by: The Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra
The Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra presents: Fiesta! Fiesta! 🔥
This winter, the heat arrives early.
Join the Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra for a spectacular night of rhythm, colour, and irresistible Latin groove as the country’s national jazz orchestra joins forces with an all-star lineup of Aotearoa’s finest musicians from the Latin music community.
From the pulse of Afro-Cuban rhythms to the fire of salsa and the lush sounds of Latin jazz, Fiesta! Fiesta! is a celebration of the rich musical traditions of Latin America — brought vividly to life by the extraordinary talent living here in Aotearoa.
Expect soaring brass, driving percussion, and music that will lift you out of the depths of winter and onto your feet.
Warm the heart. Move the body. Celebrate the music.

events.long_description:
Communication English - Level 3
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Have increased your confidence in speaking English
• Be able to understand native speakers more
• Have improved your vocabulary and grammar
• Understand and use informal spoken English
• Understand and describe more about local systems and customs
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents but to enrol you must contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) for an interview. There are limited free places available and you may need to go on a waiting list. events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $79 with ID, all others pay $149).
An opportunity to learn basic practical skills to assist you in learning to communicate in an assertive healthy manner. Come prepared to participate in class, as well as be working on assigned activities between classes. We'll work both individually and as a group using this environment to help us grow as we learn.
  By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
Use a strategy to identify your own feelings, and to express what you would like
Have an increased understanding of your own and other peoples’ anger, dealing with that in a more assertive manner
Use strategies to allow you not to be manipulated by other people
Speak in a way that commands respect and to begin to learn how to say “no”
Begin interacting more effectively in your personal relationships and the workplace
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

For those who have done Introductory 2 or equivalent, this level assumes confidence in using basic spoken language (e.g. greetings, directions, use of numbers, shopping, ordering food and drink and talking about the weather).

• Organise an outing: invite / accept / refuse an invitation (giving reasons)

(NZ Citizens and residents pay $210 with ID, all others pay $379).
Learn all the skills and strategies you need to succeed in the IELTS examination.
Bring pen and paper.
By the end of the course you will have:
Improved tone, fluency and accuracy of speech with relevance to IELTS
Extended relevant vocabulary through practice of Reading and Writing tasks
Improved accuracy in academic and general language structure, through step-by-step guidance in essay writing
Reviewed key grammatical points which are useful for writing
Become familiar with various types of questions in the reading module and learned strategies to enable success
Improved listening skills through regular practice and familiarisation with listening tasks

events.name: Writing a Novel
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Writing a Novel
An introductory course for those keen to start writing a novel. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Generate ideas for writing your novel
• Create convincing characters
• Write compelling dialogue
• Develop a strong structure for your story
• Follow the process towards completing your first draft 
Bring pen and paper.
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers' work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.

events.id: Understanding and Supporting Your Neurodivergent Child: A Practical Parent Course-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Understanding and Supporting Your Neurodivergent Child: A Practical Parent Course
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
This course is designed for parents and caregivers of neurodivergent tamariki who want practical, evidence-informed strategies to better support their child’s wellbeing, behaviour, and participation at home and school.
Across three sessions, we will explore the foundations of neurodiversity, including different neurotypes, strengths-based perspectives, and neuroaffirming language. You will gain insight into sensory processing, regulation, and the critical role of connection and co-regulation in supporting your child.
The course also provides guidance on navigating Aotearoa New Zealand’s health, education, and disability systems, including how to access supports and advocate effectively for your child. We will explore common challenges such as school refusal, burnout, and meltdowns, with practical strategies for prevention, in-the-moment support, and recovery.
There will be opportunities for discussion, reflection, and shared learning, as well as a Q&A-style panel session incorporating both professional and lived experience perspectives.
This is a group-based learning environment (not individual therapy), with an emphasis on practical tools, connection, and empowerment.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Storytelling On Stage-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Storytelling On Stage
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Storytelling On Stage
Do you love storytelling shows like The Moth? Come join the growing art of live non-fiction storytelling. Learn how to find ideas from your life, craft a compelling narrative, and deliver your personal story in a confident and entertaining way.
 Website description:
Live storytelling is an artform unto itself, requiring different skills and techniques from standup comedy or prose writing. In this four-part workshop, you’ll learn:
- Creative writing exercises and daily practices to to find meaningful stories from your life
- Tools for crafting a compelling personal narrative
- Techniques to build suspense, drama, and humour into your stories
- Stagecraft and performance skills to heighten and polish your delivery
By the end of the class you'll have
- One or more polished 5-10 minute stories to showcase on stage
- Dozens of ideas to expand upon for future stories
- Writing practices to improve your life as a creative
- Less stage fright and more confidence as a speaker
 NOTE: You will be telling true stories from your life in front of the class, building to an (optional) LIVE graduation storytelling showcase at a local venue!
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate
How often have you said, ‘I wish I could write’? There are many different kinds of writing. Starting is easy so why not give it a go by joining this creative writing ‘tasting plate’.
Each week we’ll focus on a different area of fiction or creative non-fiction. By the end of the programme, you’ll have tried your hand at writing a short story, a narrative poem, a travel memoir, a personal essay drawing from your life, an interview article, a story for children, a short film script and a piece of historical fiction.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Create works of fiction 
• Develop creative non-fiction text 
• Explore the natural inner spirit of writing 
• Discover your unique writer’s voice 
• Reflect on your own experiences in writing 
• Investigate different writing strategies
• Discover where your personal writing passions sit and what works for you.
Bring pen and paper or a laptop. 
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Poetry Lab-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Poetry Lab
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Poetry Lab
This course is ideal for poets of all levels hoping to improve their writing skills, develop their voice and get feedback on their work. Each class will include a short lecture of things to think about during the session but the rest of the time will be spent workshopping your poems.
Bring pen and paper to all sessions.
What we'll cover:
Week 1 - Introduction: experiencing poetry and getting to know your peers
Week 2 - Finding Inspiration
Week 3 - Avoiding the cliché
Week 4 - Bringing out your voice
Week 5 - Embracing the page

events.id: Exploring our Shared Humanity-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Exploring our Shared Humanity
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join experienced psychotherapist Rohit Grover for a six-week course that invites you to look beyond the surface of everyday life. In these two-hour sessions, we will explore the core tenets of existential therapy, moving away from the conventional focus on self and hedonism. Instead, we'll grapple with life's big questions:
What does it truly mean to live a value-driven life?
Do our personal values matter, or is the search for meaning just an illusion?
What is the "being" part of "human being" all about?
Through guided discussions, reflective exercises, and insights from classic existential thinkers, Rohit will help you navigate this profound territory. This isn't about finding easy answers. It's about developing the courage to ask the right questions, to confront the realities of freedom, responsibility, and meaning, and to discover a more authentic way of living.
This course is for anyone ready to step off the treadmill and begin a deeper exploration of what it means to be alive. Come and find out what it means to truly exist
Upon completion of this course, participants will be able to:
Differentiate between a life driven by external pressures or fleeting pleasures and one rooted in personal values and authentic meaning.
Identify and critically examine the core existential givens of human existence, such as freedom, responsibility, isolation, and the search for meaning.
Articulate a personal understanding of what it means to "be" a human being, moving beyond a purely functional or biological definition.
Arrive at sensing rather than thinking. Sensing this existence, this dance of life. Widen your view of existence.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Mindfulness Meditation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mindfulness Meditation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mindfulness Meditation
Meditation can help us with life’s challenges and is a wise way to work with strong emotions and stress, as well as enlivening the ordinary times. It’s simple and doesn’t need any special resources. Often learning to meditate is presented as something esoteric and mysterious but really it should be a straightforward thing based on our everyday experience.
Come along to this course and learn how to put a meditation practice in place in your life. You’ll be introduced to some different meditation and mindfulness techniques and get an insight into the modern psychology and the ancient wisdom that supports a meditation practice.
The four sessions will provide time to practise different styles of meditation, lots of hints about a fruitful approach to take to set up a regular practice in your life and opportunities to discuss progress with others in the group.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Describe some ways in which modern psychology and ancient wisdom support meditation
Understand what you need to do to make time for meditation in your life
Describe practical tips to make meditation fruitful
Practise different kinds of guided and silent meditation
Find resources (books, on-line information, other classes and retreats) to support your meditation
Bring a notepad and pen. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Qi Gong-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Qi Gong
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Qi Gong
Qigong has both psychological and physical components and involves the regulation of the mind, breath, and body’s movement and posture.
In most forms of qigong:
Breath is slow, long, and deep. Breath patterns may switch from abdominal breathing to breathing combined with speech sounds.
Movements are typically gentle and smooth, aimed for relaxation.
Mind regulation includes focusing one’s attention and visualization.
Dynamic (active) qigong techniques primarily focus on body movements, especially movements of the whole body or arms and legs. Meditative (passive) qigong techniques can be practiced in any posture that can be maintained over time and involve breath and mind exercises, with almost no body movement.
Wear loose, comfortable clothing and bring a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Crochet for Calmness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Crochet for Calmness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crochet for Calmness
The repetitive movements of crochet help the brain make more serotonin, which can decrease tension and feelings of depression. We’ll move through all the steps from the starting slipknot to reading patterns and diagrams and making your first crochet projects.
You’ll learn to make a granny square, change colours and how to increase and decrease. You’ll also find out more about the notations and abbreviations of crochet stitches so you can make those awesome crochet projects you've been waiting to make!
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Discuss the benefits of crochet for relaxation and alleviation of mood
• Read a basic crochet pattern and recognise crochet terminology
• Choose yarns and hooks for projects
• Use a range of stitches with confidence
• Complete at least one project of your choice (granny square, keychain, garden decoration)
Suitable for all beginners, both left and right-handers and those who may have struggled to learn to crochet. Initial materials provided but please bring along any yarn or crochet hooks you would like to work with.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: How to be a DJ - An Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: How to be a DJ - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
How to be a DJ - An Introduction
Always wanted to try being a DJ but not sure where to begin? Experienced and professional DJ Shan (Shannon Chng-Rawiri) will teach you all about the technical aspects, equipment and terminology, as well as the industry in general, and enable you to put together a confident short performance.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Gain an understanding of the work content and career opportunities in this industry
Learn the difference between what a DJ is required to deliver in the following situations: Mobile, Club, Radio, Turntablist and Hobby
Participate in various practical activities: Slip Cueing and Baby Scratch, basic beat matching and song structures
Gain a good understanding of the equipment used and terminology including: - Tables: pitch control - Mixer: crossfader, input faders, EQ, gain, cue system - Needles: back tracking - Slip Mats
Learn about Counting Bars and Matching Tempos - The difference between a Bar, Phrases and BPM - Techniques to Count, Hand position and movement etc
Confidently present a 5 minute performance in class
The course will be held at DJ Shan’s studio at Shop 21, 73 Cuba Street, Wellington.  Bring headphones, a USB with your favourite music and a laptop, if you have one. Limited to eight participants.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
Elevate your photography from ordinary to extraordinary and ensure your work stands out in the digital age. Creating captivating photographs and understanding what makes a photograph engaging is something every photographer should master. Camera settings are essential, but once you know them, you need to learn how to take photographs that stir emotions, impact your audience, and leave a lasting impression. After all, isn’t that the true purpose of photography? People don’t want to see the same landscapes or flowers they’ve seen thousands of times. But how do you turn what you love into something that makes people go, "Wow"?
Captivation in photography means stopping a viewer in their tracks, making them look, feel, and connect. It’s about delivering a photograph that enthrals, evokes profound emotions, and shifts perspectives.
In a world brimming with images, only a few have the power to truly captivate and linger in our memories.
 This pioneering course is your gateway to mastering the art of creating captivating photographs at a time when AI-generated images are saturating our visual landscape. Create something genuine and memorable—images that spark curiosity, evoke emotions, and inspire changes in perception. It's time to define your unique style, refine your vision, and make your mark.
 Course Outcomes
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
Identify the foundational elements that transform an ordinary image into a captivating one.
Create photographs that make people say "Wow" by applying techniques that add a spark of wonder to your shots.
Infuse your photographs with curiosity, making each frame a source of intrigue and interest.
Understand why some images linger in memory and how to make your photographs unforgettable.
Develop a unique style and vision that sets your work apart in the crowded digital landscape.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Chess is an ancient game of strategy and analysis that hones concentration and focus, as you master the skills allowing you to win at this competitive recreation.
Join tutor, Ryan Hill to learn how to play this game from the beginning.
You will quickly go from understanding the basics to being able to engage in playing confidently and discover where to go for further training or to carry on playing – both in person and online.
Over 600 million people play this game that has remained popular throughout the ages – why don’t you become one of them!
Bring pen and paper to take notes.
By the end of this course you will be able to:
*Use all of the chess pieces to play the game
*Recount and use some of the most common openings and traps
*Recognise where to go for further training or to play
*Develop increasing levels of confidence in your ability to play and possibly win

events.id: Garden Planting Design-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Garden Planting Design
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Garden Planting Design
Spent a fortune on plants and still not achieved the dream? Can’t work out why some plants thrive and others barely survive? Ready to make exciting changes but no idea where to start and not much gardening background? This interactive and fun course will answer all your queries and give you confidence to create the garden you want with the right plants to achieve it. Style of garden, colour, plant palette – we’ll cover it all – and more.

Time permitting, we will look at photos of your gardens as “personal case studies” to help you get the most from the course.
After completing the course, you will have created a detailed hand drawn plant design that will be your blue print for the next steps… planting up!
 
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
•Identify the style of your dream garden and learn what plants will best achieve this and guarantee success
• Identify and work with the site and climate conditions of your garden/outdoor areas to be developed; modify plant choices if necessary
• Accurately draw your detailed planting design on paper and to scale.
• Understand the role of colour and different plants in the garden and incorporate it into your design
• Learn how to improve your soil to guarantee success
• Use your final design to create a “shopping list” of plants
• Calculate anticipated expenditure / prepare a budget (to also include soil improving items)
 
Please note that this course DOES NOT include:
 
Hardscaping design. This is available in a separate course.
 
• Planting up your garden
• Recommending suppliers, service providers and retailers, although knowledge and experiences of the tutor and peers will be shared appropriately
• Pruning practices or plant disease management
• Organic methods of practice, although knowledge and experiences of the tutor and peers will be shared appropriately
• In depth coverage of specific plant groups eg roses, vegetables
Please bring some scrap paper, a notebook, pens, pencils and an eraser.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
events.description:
Can’t work out why some plants thrive and others barely survive?
Ready to make exciting changes but no idea where to start and not much gardening background?
Time permitting, we will look at photos of your gardens as “personal case studies” to help you get the most from the course.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: •Identify the style of your dream garden and learn what plants will best achieve this and guarantee success • Identify and work with the site and climate conditions of your garden/outdoor areas to be developed; modify plant choices if necessary • Accurately draw your detailed planting design on paper and to scale.
This is available in a separate course.

Smartphone videography is an increasingly important and useful skillset. events.id: Wellington Opera — Manon-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Wellington Opera — Manon
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
For its 2026 season, Wellington Opera brings one of the most popular and enduring French operas to life. Manon by Jules Massenet will feature Kiwi-Samoan tenor Pene Pati and Kiwi-Egyptian soprano Amina Edris. Joining this powerhouse duo is another New Zealander, baritone Phillip Rhodes will sing the role of Lescaut. Manon tells the tragic love story of Manon Lescaut and the Chevalier des Grieux. The story unfolds in five acts, exploring themes of love, desire, and the consequences of choices made in pursuit of pleasure. 
Pene Pati is widely known for his Sol3 Mio fame. He is a recent New Zealand Arts Laureate and is the first Samoan tenor to perform on the world’s top stages, including the Opéra national de Paris, Covent Garden, London and the Metropolitan Opera, New York.

Homemade egg noodles topped with your favourite sauce creates a tasty meal. Learn how to create Chinese style egg noodles that can serve as the basis for many traditional noodle dishes.  Create soft meat buns in the traditional Chinese style. Filled with succulent and juicy meat filling, these buns are perfect for a simple breakfast, lunch or snack.

make a selection of Chinese egg noodles and buns to take home
understand more about the Chinese egg noodle-making process
understand more about the Chinese bun-making process
feel more confident to make Chinese egg noodles and buns by hand at home

events.id: Creative Problem-Solving Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Problem-Solving Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Problem-Solving Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Through engaging puzzles, group activities, and proven creativity techniques, you'll discover how to tackle challenges from new angles and find solutions you might never have considered. Learn to break through mental blocks, generate innovative ideas, and lead groups to make better decisions together. This workshop draws on Neil's background in teaching, test-preparation, and creative thinking to help you unlock your natural problem-solving abilities in a supportive environment.
What You'll Get From This Workshop:
·       Simple techniques to generate ideas individually or in a group: brainstorming, mind-mapping, and more
·       Practice solving puzzles that sharpen your lateral thinking skills
·       Learn how to overcome blocks and restrictive thinking patterns
·       Experience effective group leading methods you can use with your own teams
·       Discover how to identify root causes of problems instead of just treating symptoms
·       Take home specific tools for approaching problems differently
·       Build confidence in leading problem-solving sessions with others
Who This Workshop Is For: This workshop is for anyone who faces challenges at work, leads meetings or teams, manages projects, or simply wants to become a better problem solver. Perfect for team leaders, community organizers, and anyone who's ever been stuck in an unproductive meeting wondering "there must be a better way!" No special background needed—just bring your curiosity and willingness to try new approaches.
What to Expect: A day filled with interactive activities, problem-solving challenges, and practical techniques you can use right away. You'll learn proven steps and while practicing facilitation skills to help groups make decisions more effectively. Neil creates a relaxed atmosphere where everyone feels comfortable contributing ideas and experimenting with new approaches. You'll leave with specific tools you can apply to your own challenges, plus handouts to help you remember what you've learned.
Neil's unique skill as an educator is his rare ability to blend evidence-based teaching techniques with the perfect dash of humor and storytelling, creating a learning environment where complex concepts become accessible and new skills feel immediately applicable to your real-world challenges.
  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Crime Writing - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crime Writing - An Introduction
Step into the world of crime writing and discover how to build stories that grip readers from page one. Whether you’re brand new to writing or already have a few drafts under your belt, this course will sharpen your skills and push your storytelling further.
 Bring a notebook and pens—you’ll need them.
 Across the sessions, you’ll explore:
Writing fundamentals that keep prose tight and compelling.
Crime genres and what makes each tick.
Heroes, villains, and everyone caught between.
Research techniques that make your fiction feel authentic.
Plotting, story arcs, and whether outlines are your best friend—or sworn enemy.
How to craft loglines, taglines, and blurbs that hook readers instantly.
 Designed and taught by Cat Connor, internationally published crime writer with a shelf full of thrillers, this course is equal parts practical and fun. Expect a fast pace, plenty of exercises, and tools you can carry into any writing project.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Map out a crime novel or short story.
Create believable, layered characters.
Spot (and fix) passive writing.
Build solid research habits.
Use plot diagrams to tighten structure.
Write sharp loglines, taglines, and blurbs.
 Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Knitting for Beginners
Come and reap the benefits of a mindful and meditative craft-form.  Meet other people interested in making things with their hands, chat over crafting and find out what the craft craze is all about.
Join crafter Stella Carruthers as she shares her ten years plus knitting experience in this beginner’s knitting class that supports each student as an individual learner.
Master basic knitting techniques, learn how to cast stitches on and off the needles, explore the knit and purl stitches and practice how to hold the needles in a way that is comfortable for you - then learn how to knit in the English style.
Knitting needles and yarn are included in the course fee, but you're welcome to bring along your own 4-ply yarn if you wish. 
By the end of the course you will have created two squares of knitting, one in basic garter stitch and the other utilising both knit and purl stitches to create the knitted fabric called stocking stitch.

events.id: South Summit ‘RUN IT BACK’ New Zealand Tour | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: South Summit ‘RUN IT BACK’ New Zealand Tour | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
South Summit are soon to release their sophomore album, RUN IT BACK on June 12 and to celebrate, they have announced RUN IT BACK NZ TOUR.
Coming to Aotearoa this July and August the band will take on Queenstown hotspot Yonder on Thursday 30 July, The Loons, Lyttleton on Friday 31 July, San Fran, Wellington on Saturday 1 August and Auckland’s Double Whammy on Sunday 2 August. 
Run It Back marks the band's second album, featuring stand out singles ‘TOP OF THE HILL’ and ‘ON THE DASH’ which have sent strong signals toward the confident evolution of their sound. The twelve-track record  represents a period of experimentation for the band; one where they found themselves pulled towards threads of funk, hip hop and R&B, while also driven by a desire to galvanise their signature blend of reggae and rock.
In 2024, they released their highly anticipated debut album The Bliss, a reggae-infused indie rock record that showcased both tight songwriting and a willingness to experiment across styles. It debuted at #2 on the ARIA Album Charts and propelled the band into a new tier, with heavy Triple J support, major festival slots and international touring, including appearances at Falls Festival, Groovin The Moo, SXSW Sydney, Vivid LIVE Debut at the Sydney Opera House plus a huge Australasian headline tour.
After a recent visit to Aotearoa to play at Six 60’s new Country & Roots Festival Sweet Home Aotearoa, South Summit now return to New Zealand for their third headline tour in the country. These four shows promise the bands’ renowned live charisma and classic fan favourites, whilst welcoming fans into a brand new era.

events.name: Beervana
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description: Based at Hnry Stadium on Wellington’s magnificent waterfront, Beervana attracts over 16,000 people over two days. About 50 breweries set up shop on the covered concourse that surrounds the stadium. They represent a selection of beer brewers from around New Zealand, Australia, and further afield. Revellers can make a full circuit of the building and not miss anything. Visit stalls, taste some uniquely brewed beers, chat to the brewers, and catch up with mates. Adding to the atmosphere are food stalls and multiple live music performances. There are four sessions spread across the weekend. Each runs for five hours so there is plenty of time to enjoy the vibe, learn something new, and discover an amazing brew.

events.id: Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World
This workshop will be a confronting one that will expose you to the risks that children face in this modern overindulgent world. Many have become used to being the constant centre of attention and are unable to delay gratification and aren’t competent in everyday skills. They have few self-care skills and skills relating to others, can’t take personal responsibility and don’t know what is enough or what is “normal” for others – these children end up having a difficult time of it in life and you will too.
 You’ll take away new insight and knowledge plus strategies that you can apply that very night to help your child to be a responsible, likeable and respectful person.
By the end of the session, you’ll be able to:
• Reflect on the current context parents are faced with today
• Find the relevant research
• Practise the parenting tools and discuss the model
• Know where to go to get support
Aimed at parents, grandparents and caregivers of children of all ages.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
events.description:
Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World This workshop will be a confronting one that will expose you to the risks that children face in this modern overindulgent world.
Many have become used to being the constant centre of attention and are unable to delay gratification and aren’t competent in everyday skills.
They have few self-care skills and skills relating to others, can’t take personal responsibility and don’t know what is enough or what is “normal” for others – these children end up having a difficult time of it in life and you will too.
You’ll take away new insight and knowledge plus strategies that you can apply that very night to help your child to be a responsible, likeable and respectful person.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.

events.id: Alan Davies — Think Ahead Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Alan Davies — Think Ahead Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Critically acclaimed stand-up comedian, actor, writer, and TV personality Alan Davies is heading to Wellington in 2026. His new show, ‘Think Ahead’, marks his first visit to Aotearoa in a decade. Like the latest instalment of his memoirs, White Male Stand-Up, the performance covers his extensive career, and the highs and lows he’s experienced. Alan says the show is about a late middle-aged stand-up comic. He thinks he’s Marty McFly, but he’s older than Doc Brown. He spends more time in the pharmacy than the gym and is subjected to relentless eye rolls from his kids. 
Davies is a staple of British television. He’s known for his performance as the eponymous hero in the long-running TV series Jonathan Creek, and as a permanent panellist on QI. His talk show, Alan Davies: As Yet Untitled, recently reached its seventh season. He is also one of the UK’s best-loved comedians.

events.id: Nurse Georgie Carroll: Infectious-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Nurse Georgie Carroll: Infectious
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The delivery is razor sharp, and the comedy is not so much deadpan as bedpan. Nurse Georgie Carroll is back with a brand-new show. For Infectious, Georgie turns 20 years as a frontline surgical nurse into a stand-up that’s full of laughs, shocks, and heart. She is known as a natural storyteller whose style is authentic, fearless, and deeply human. It blends personal anecdotes, healthcare absurdities, and everyday family chaos into sharp, relatable observations. It’s big-hearted, no-nonsense, and riotous. 
Beyond the stage, Georgie Carroll is also an author whose writing captures the same warmth and humour that define her stand-up. Her stories celebrate resilience, laughter, and the humanity within healthcare, inspiring audiences across professions.

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making Intensive - Beginners' Workshop
Learn the essential skills and techniques needed to make your own creations. The morning will begin with simple design examples and techniques using specialist jewellers’ tools, working with copper or brass. In the afternoon you will form and craft your own ring from sterling silver.
 All materials included in course fee.  Please bring pen and paper and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.  Limited to nine participants. Please note you cannot be late for this one-day course, you must be there from the start of the course when health and safety matters are covered.

By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Create a silver band 
• Demonstrate techniques for working sterling silver 
• Perform metal forming, piercing and texturing skills 
• Use essential jewellery-making tools accurately

Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives
There is a widespread ‘lack of confidence’ epidemic among creatives, and if you have ever felt like your work is not good enough, hesitated to submit your art to competitions, or doubted your skills when applying for creative jobs, you are not alone! Creatives often feel imposter syndrome most acutely. Unlike other industries, those in the creative field produce something new every time, and sharing this with the world can be daunting, even for established artists.
 This pervasive self-doubt, known as imposter syndrome, affects countless artists and creators, often holding them back from realising their full potential. This concise workshop is designed to help you uncover the roots of this inner voice of insecurity and provide you with effective tools to overcome it. Through engaging activities and practical exercises, you will learn how to transform self-doubt into a source of strength, empowering you to confidently share your unique talents with the world.
 What you won't find in this masterclass: This is not a session about general self-growth or self-confidence, and it does not include positive affirmations or self-talk. All the information provided is based on scientific research and is applicable to all creative sectors.
Bring along some examples of your work on the day (eg a painting, a photograph of your work, etc).
 By the end of this workshop, you’ll be able to: 
Identify and challenge the origins of your imposter syndrome.
Apply practical techniques to silence your inner critic.
Share your creative work with increased confidence.
Turn self-doubt into a motivational tool.
Recognise and celebrate your achievements as a creative professional.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pelvic Power Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pelvic Power Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pelvic Power Workshop
Apart from assisting with supporting internal organs, and controlling elimination, the pelvic floor is an important part of a woman's overall stability and posture, so you will gain improvement in this respect as well. You'll learn far more than conventional pelvic floor strengthening. 
You'll learn to differentiate different parts of the pelvic floor, and also how the pelvic floor functions in conjunction with breathing and spinal movements.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Recognise the anatomy of the pelvic floor, where the muscles are and how they work
• Coordinate the pelvic floor muscles with breathing, and with the spine, and recognise its role in overall good posture
• Discover what habits you have which work against your pelvic floor muscles working well
• Practise exercises designed to help you rehabilitate and strengthen your pelvic floor
Please note that instructions are given verbally rather than by demonstration, so good hearing and understanding of English is required. For women only.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Atsuko Okatsuka — The Big Bowl Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Atsuko Okatsuka — The Big Bowl Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s been described as having one-of-a-kind humour, boundless energy, and infectious charm. Comedian and actress Atsuko Okatsuka is heading to Wellington with her latest show, ‘The Big Tour’.   
With podcasts, television and film acting, and standup specials on streaming platforms, Atsuko has gained a huge following. Her previous tour, ‘Full Grown’, sold out over 200 shows across 20 countries. Her latest standup special, ‘Father’, premiered on Disney+ and has made it onto its Top 10 list. 
Atsuko’s viral moments are as legendary as her stand-up. She has ridden a Jeepney (a type of public utility vehicle) in the Philippines and started the #DropChallenge, which helped bring Beyoncé’s ‘Partition’ back to the charts. She’s become a true queen of the internet with clips from her live-filmed show, The Intruder, racking up over 100 million views. Fans even turn up to her shows wearing wigs styled as her signature bowl cut as a form of homage. Humour has no borders when you’re that funny.

events.name: Cooking: Fill the Tins - Kiwi Baking
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Fill the tins -Traditional kiwi baking.
Our grandmothers would never be caught without a selection of home-baked biscuits, a slice or two and at least one type of cake or loaf. Today many of those skills are no longer being passed on.
In this workshop you will get back to basics with some of the Kiwi baking classics which, made well, are delicious and widely loved.
Pastry chef, Sarah Mackenzie will teach you the techniques to make:
A light and moist Banana Cake,
A pretty, delicate Louise Cake and
melt in the mouth Afghans.
All ingredients included in course fee - bring some tins to fill!
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pub Choir — Just Think About It-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Pub Choir — Just Think About It
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
You’ll definitely feel better if you come and have a sing with a few thousand strangers. That’s what New Zealand-born vocalist, conductor, and composer Astrid Jorgensen thinks. Astrid is the brainchild behind the touring show Pub Choir — Just Think About It. The premise is simple — come to the Michael Fowler Centre on 19 August and learn one popular song in three-part harmony. What began as a small gathering in a pub has now grown to fill concert halls around the world. No prior training is required — just bring your voice and a sense of humour. 
Astrid established Pub Choir in Brisbane in 2017. Her work associated with the show has gone on to win her several awards. This includes one of the 40 Under 40 Most Influential Asian-Australians by the Asian-Australian Leadership Summit in 2021 and a Medal of the Order of Australia (OAM) in 2023.

events.id: Adobe InDesign - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Adobe InDesign - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Adobe InDesign
This introduction to Adobe's InDesign program will cover: setting preferences; understanding the frame-based approach through exploration of frame creation, properties and use; placing and editing text (with styles); placing graphics; defining and applying colour and print considerations.
By the end of the workshop, you'll have been introduced to InDesign. You'll be shown the program and the main features of its desktop, and then be taken through how to create and work with the main publishing items (text and graphics) in order to create your own publication. 
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course you'll be able to:
• Describe the features of InDesign and how to work with them
• Create text frames, connect text frames and format text
• Create shapes and frames and format them
• Import pictures into publications
• Use rulers, guides and the pasteboard to lay out pages
• Finish off, check and then print 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Cooking: Mumbai Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Mumbai Street Food
Indian street food varies from region to region but wherever you are, you’ll find a large selection of scrumptious snacks often prepared in front of your eyes. Manjula will show you how to recreate the authentic flavours of Mumbai, using a range of local and imported ingredients.
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, tea towels and a dish cloth and containers to take any leftovers home.
By the end of the workshop you’ll be able to:
• Prepare a range of popular Indian street foods
• Follow the recipes provided and prepare them under supervision
• Describe more about classic Indian flavours and ingredients
• Identify local retail outlets where you can buy specialty ingredients 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Zentangle® Method - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zentangle® Method - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zentangle® Method - Beginners
The Zentangle® method is an easy-to-learn, relaxing and fun method to create amazing images by drawing structured patterns.
In this course you will learn to make beautiful art, following the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method from a certified Zentangle teacher.
Discover your creative side, even if you think you don’t have one. You might surprise yourself!  If you can hold a pen, you can do this.
Creating Zentangle patterns is super relaxing and a creative form of mindfulness. Increase your awareness and focus, get absorbed and feel more confident while making your own unique and original art.
Learn about borders, strings, and shading. Learn your first tangles and discover the world of Crescent Moon, Hollibaugh, Printemps, and Florz. It feels great to have creativity flowing. Anything is possible one stroke at a time. There are no mistakes to make here!
After a step-by-step introduction you will take home your amazing images and have discovered a new way to relax.
All art materials are included in course fee.  Tea and coffee is available but please bring your lunch/snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-    follow the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method.
-    make beautiful structured black and white drawings.
-    explain why creating Zentangle patterns is a relaxing form of creative mindfulness.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Indian Homestyle-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Indian Homestyle
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Indian Homestyle
Delicious traditional Indian dishes (not based on ghee and cream!) are the focus in this four-session course where Manjula will share with you the recipes, flavours and aromas she grew up with.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Discuss the flavours and spices used in Indian and Gujarati cuisine
• Know where to purchase ingredients for a selection of vegetarian and meat dishes and finger food
• Read and follow a range of recipes step-by-step
• Create a range of classic dishes from Southern and Northern India as cooked at home
All ingredients included in course fee.
Bring an apron, dishcloth, and a couple of tea towels and four containers with lids to take any leftovers home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Dita Von Teese-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Dita Von Teese
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With her signature mystery and old-Hollywood glamour, Dita Von Teese is a global icon in the world of burlesque. She’s bringing an exciting new show to The Opera House in August. ‘Nocturnelle’ is a sensual spectacle inspired by the golden age of magic. It blends sophisticated striptease with the art of illusion for an unforgettable evening. Dita is accompanied by an ensemble of hand-picked neo-burlesque artists, so expect to be dazzled. 
Dita Von Teese is considered to have set the benchmark for American burlesque. The exotic and provocative art form has advanced under her unrivalled artistry and flair. The performer, creative director, and choreographer has sold out some of the world’s grandest venues, from Shanghai to New York. She is also a designer, collaborator, and entrepreneur. She’s forayed into fashion, beauty, music, and publishing.

events.id: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Award-winning animated children’s series, Kiri and Lou, follows the adventures of Kiri, a feisty little dinosaur, and her best friend Lou. The New Zealand made show explores the world of feelings through laughter, song and play. Now, the songs from the show come to life in delicious orchestral colour thanks to the players of Orchestra Wellington.
Presented by Liv Tennet, the voice of Kiri, this concert brings the music of one of Aotearoa’s favourite TV shows to life. Beautiful visuals from some of the episodes will be projected during the concert. This is a family-friendly event and is recommended for children aged 3 and above.
This handcrafted stop-motion animation show is now in its third season. Kiri and Lou shows in many countries around the world including Sweden, Czech Republic, Canada, Mexico, United States, and Latvia.

events.id: Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food
Come and try some delicious new recipes - you don’t have to have done Mei-Fen's first course to join in!
Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Follow recipes using correct procedures
• Create a selection of tasty Singapore Hawker dishes, which may includes recipes such as Chicken Rendang and Nasi Lemak.
• Feel more confident to cook Singapore Hawker Food at home
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
When comedian Chris Parker was young, he liked attention. He’s always endeavoured to make people look at him. In his latest show, he continues the theme of a lifetime. Take a Good Hard Look at Me displays the Kiwi comic’s sharp wit and clever timing. Chris says of his hour-long show — “There’s no secrets about to be dished, no valuable lesson to take away. I’m just a 35-year-old gay man, who was told there was something special about him as a kid and has been grappling with that ever since.” 
Chris is an actor, comedian, writer, and podcaster. He gained fame on TV’s Have You Been Paying Attention and Thank God You’re Here. He has written and performed extensively with Tom Sainsbury on live shows such as The Opening Night Before Christmas, Camping, Giggly Gerties, and Hauraki Horror. He also created and performed several sketch comedy shows with fellow comedian Hayley Sproull, including Tighty Whiteys, The Outsiders’ Guide, and Milky Bits.
